Comfortable high waist fit. Wish there was a longer inseam option. At 5’ 10” she would normally use a 32” inseam. Although this pant fits her very nicely in all aspects except length ( I know, the yoga length is a shorter style) she just doesn’t feel comfortable with the high water look. We do have a daughter that these will fit much better so we will share them with her next month.I like the construction. The wide waist band is supportive without cutting into her stomach.Fabric is soft and stretchy and stitching in the construction seems to be well done.

Wonderful pants These pants are absolutely impeccable. Superb workmanship and perfect fit. I received size XS 29” inseam. I am 5’3” 117 lb, 35” hip. The leg is a wide straight cut, but not so wide that it is floppy. I don’t usually buy wide leg pants but these are proportioned correctly for my size. The wide waistband does not roll down. These pants have 4 pockets that lay flat and do not stick out or add bulk. The smooth wrinkle free fabric is slightly heavy (not thin or see thru) making them dress or casual 3 season wear. Fall, winter, spring. iPhone fits completely in either front pocket and does not fall out when sitting. I highly recommend these pants.

Good quality, beautiful color and great fit I am 5’8” 132-135 lbs athletically build and ordered a size Small. These pants are just the right length for me, hit at the ankle, if you are taller than 5’8” these will be ankle cropped for you. They fit great through the leg, definitely straight fit, but are loose in the waist for me (my waist is 26”), but a belt will fix that. Overall quality is great, the material is matte and has a subtle leather “grain” printed on it. The color is a nice deep cool chocolate brown. The fabric is on the thinner side and my biggest complaint is the zipper - it’s flimsy and gets stuck, I bet it will break. Overall, definitely recommend, pleasantly surprised. Oh, I did have to wait 2-3 weeks for them, that was annoying.

Comfy but Not Ideal for Taller Girls Being taller than the average woman, my wife was keen to order these “long” pants with hope for a reasonable fit. At the time of ordering, there were no length measurements although there is a size chart shown now. She went with medium based on what she would normally order based on her 6’ height and 34” inseam but the leg length is really not designed for those with longer legs. The pants are still very comfy to wear and the waist band is thick and well made. She’ll mostly be wearing these for lounging around the house but with consideration to the leg length, she gives these 4 stars.

Yellow is mustard, fit might run small for some Personally I ordered the yellow because it’s mustard!!!! I can see where some might say it runs small as there’s not much difference between sizes…. Im about a size 12 in most skinny jeans/jeggings (my waist is about 33-34” & hips 43” (I have kind of a flat butt)) & I think I’ll keep both the 12 & 14 unless the 12 shrinks in the wash a ton! 12 fits a bit tighter (but not uncomfortably so) & 14 more roomy comfort. Even tho I’m only 5’3, i’m short waisted (I don’t find the rise too high like mom jeans), & my legs are longer (33” inseam) & these are the perfect length on me! Folded inseam 25”, unfolded 27”. Fit is more of a slim straight leg pant which works well for my more athletic build legs/hips. Curvier gals may want to size up another size to accommodate any hips or butt? The fabric has a little stretch (97% cotton, 3% elastane) so I don’t anticipate it to stretch out much while being worn but I will update the review if I notice that!

Fits like a sausage casing! The fabric and construction are very nice on these pants. Made in Bangladesh from 95% Cotton, 5% Elastane, the fabric is a decent thickness, the buttons are well sewn and it even has a full metal YKK zipper. The fit is where these lost stars for me. They are super tight in the waist on me. Really they are tight all over but they are most uncomfortable in the waist. The length is ok on me, in fact if I uncuff them I could even wear them as a full length pant if they were not so tight! For size reference I am 5'4" 150lbs, pear shaped and typically wear a size 8 or 10 in pants. In the picture I am squeezed into the size 8 in black. I don’t even think the size 10 would work. These pants are also very soft and have a brushed feel to them, in black they attract lint like crazy, so consider a lighter color or keep a lint roller handy at all times!

Not my favorite I ordered a 3x based on reviews. They fit too large, but not sure a 2x would have been too tight. Wanted to give myself a little room with as many reviews saying they ripped. I have a big badonk and huge thighs. I wear a US size 20-ish. 5'5" The legs are weird. I was hoping for wide leg, but these are just cut wide everywhere and slightly less wide at the ankle, if that makes sense. They feel good on. A tad stiff, but ok. Smooth. Thick. They just are not flattering at all. Honestly spent a day hating my body after trying them on. Only kept them because I needed pants that weren't skinny jeans. And the color is gorgeous. Hit my feet, but I'm sure they will shrink up a bit in the wash. This fabric relaxes A LOT during wear, so be aware that what might fit at the beginning of the day may not by the end of the day.

Feels like lounge but looks like work. Size down! Size reference: I am 5’6” and 127 lbs. I normally wear a size 4 in jeans and can wear either small or medium in stretchy pants. Based off prior experience, I ordered small from Amazon Essentials because they tend run big. The fit of the small is still a little big, similar to what a medium in brick and mortar store brands would run. The smooth elastic waist has a little gap when I’m standing. I have shorter legs and the leg openings touch the top of my feet. I actually like the looser fit because if they were skin tight I think they would look more like sweats than slacks. Description: I really like how these feel. The stretch of yoga pants but made with better material so I can pass these as business casual with the right top. The tag will have to be cut out because the corner does scratch my lower back. Also, and this is an issue I have had from AMZ brands before, the black looks slightly faded compared to other dress pants. It makes it hard to pair with other black pieces from my closet because the difference is quite noticeable. Other than that, I like these comfy work pants. Deducted a star for color and scratchy tag. (Didn’t deduct a star for fit because the size chart does show small to be about 2 inches larger in the waist than standard small).

Great quality, comfortable and versatile These pants are really comfortable and easy to wear, my wife likes the style and fit of them. She likes how comfortable they are while still looking polished. There are no buttons, zippers or belt loops, so make sure to get your correct size. For reference she is is 5’4 and about 120lbs and got an extra small and likes how they fit, the length is perfect and the waist is snug but not uncomfortable. The material is good quality, she likes the thickness of them and thinks they will be fine as winter wear with boots and a turtleneck and can be worn with sandals and a tank top in warmer months. Overall she is really happy with them. She likes the style and fit. They are very versatile and classic bottoms that she expects to wear a lot. Her only issue is they don’t have pockets, just one small one in the back that isn’t really functional. I hope that sharing my opinion and experience here provides you some value when shopping and deciding on something like these pants, if so please let everyone know below.

I'm definitely ordering more but I will stick with my regular size next time.

LOL what these are at least 2 sizes too big According to Amazon's recommendations people who order the clothing sizes I do order these in a large. According to the size chart I would wear a large. I am 5'2" and weigh 160lbs. I ordered a medium because reviewers said these ran large. Medium is WAY too big. I'm not even convinced that a Small would be the right size. These are about 6" too long and are so baggy that they remind me of something from the 90s back when we wore pants big enough to fit two people inside of. I feel like maybe these were produced using a men's last and mislabled as for women. These are awful, they are just so large, and the polyester fabric is slightly shiny, making them feel like a high school athletic uniform in the weirdest way. The only bright side is that because they have so much polyester in them they are incredibly warm and don't breathe, so if you're somewhere cold these will keep you warm. But maybe order 2-3 sizes down.
Not as long as expected Great quality for the price, but I was hoping the length would be better based on ad photo shown. I ordered size L and I am 5’10. Doable but I wanted them even longer.

Great value and nice enough to wear in public I liked these palazzo pants so much I ordered a second pair in black and white polka dot. They are soft and drape nicely from the hip. They are a true palazzo pant, which I like, but buyers should be aware that they are "flowy." I am 5'6" and weigh 155 lbs. I normally wear an XL but got the XXL as prompted by Amazon. They are very long and almost touch the ground. They fit well but I think the XL would have been fine as they are stretchy. They don't look like a pajama bottom and you could easily wear them to run errands, grab lunch etc. Highly recommend!

Great, but inconsistent sizing Originally ordered M of the gray with roses when I was 4 mo pregnant, and they were the only thing that felt comfortable to wear. Once I got to six months pregnant, I ordered the large in the same color. However, the waistband of the large was 1 inch smaller than the waistband of the medium! (See pic. The medium is the wider pair on the bottom.) I still loved the pants, so I decided to return the large and order an extra large in a different color, the khaki with roses. I figured maybe in a different color they would actually be larger. I received the khaki ones today, and the fit is what I would’ve expected the large to be, but they are see-through. So now I am trying the gray ones again, this time in xxl, hoping at some point the larger sizes actually get bigger. Apart from the size inconsistencies, these are the comfiest lounge pants, and I highly recommend them for pregnant women! They are silky smooth and feel great against tender stretched belly skin. For size comparison, my pre-preggo size in most clothes is S/M. 5’6, 142lb, hourglass. Now I’m 166lb at 6mo pregnant. The mediums still fit, but I anticipate needing a size larger as I get closer to due date.

Best Pants for C Section Surgery and just all around comfortable lounge pants! I love these lounge pants! I'm currently 36 weeks pregnant, and with just 2 weeks to go until my C section, I've been living in these. I originally ordered 2 pairs, but I just ordered a 3rd because after my surgery I know I'll need loose pants that don't constrict my incision, and I have a feeling I'll only be wearing these at home for the first few weeks of recovery. I wear a size medium before and during pregnancy. I'm only 5' 3", so these do run a bit long on me. But I only wear them around the house with slippers so I don't mind one bit. With how loose fitting these are, I have no doubt I could wear a size small but I'm fine with them. They're loose enough that they don't constrict my waist, but I can also pull them about halfway up my preggo belly and they'll fit there comfortably, which I really need for after my C section. I wish I'd bought these for my first baby during my surgery recovery, and i highly recommend them before, during, and after having a baby even if you don't plan to have a C section. The weight I've gained during this pregnancy seems to be all baby, so I know I'll still fit comfortably in these after the baby comes. I may have to roll up the waist though. Highly recommend!

Size up I'm 6ft tall 215lbs I have a nice foundation type body... my legs are long AF I GOT THE XL my first ones and they were to short... my second ones I got 2xl and they still ran a little flood but the 3xl are perfect in length... Cute and comfy These pants are good to lounge around the house. PJ material and comfy. They are a cheap material, but that's ok due to how inexpensive they are. I don't see them lasting long tho, but time will tell. I noticed many said these are super long. I’m not sure if they changed the length on some styles, but the camo pair I got wasn’t very long for my height of 5’5. I'm wearing a size small. I don't see this being a good length on someone taller than my height. At least not this style. However, I do have long legs tho, so that may play a role as well. A little tip for people saying the string comes out when washed. Tie a small knot in the string before washing and it won’t come out. It’s easy to take the knot out after washing. Also, it's probably best to hang dry for longer wear, since it is a cheaper material. I’m middle age (52), and they are good if you get hot flashes, since the material is light. Hope this helps someone.

Different patterns= Very different types of fabric First thing you need to know is that every pattern and color has a different fabric makeup which changes the look and feel of them. This is why the reviews have huge outliers. I bought the grey polka dot to start with, and they are polyester and spandex. I got the XXL based on reviews and they are huge but cozy. They did have a bit of a cheap look (shiny fabric and exposed stitching). I decided to order the solid black in just XL and they are 15% cotton and 85% polyester! Different feeling and they are also shorter but look better quality. I am 5’ 6” and 195lbs. Just had a baby and carry my weight in my hips. The XL is fine and these things stretch. I am concerned about the exposed stitches in the grey pair. They came this way and look like they are about to rip. I’ve worn them for a week and washed them twice. They are super comfortable and I just hope they last. I am rating the black pants 5 stars.

My normal size M/L at 8 months pregnant I got the 3XL There are a lot of these same looking pants sold all over Amazon. I ordered some by the brand LUBERLIN and I definitely think the fit of these AMiERY ones are better. Be aware: The sizing on these pants is not traditional US sizing!! The 3XL runs waaaay small but not *AS* small as the LUBERLIN ones. For reference, I am 5'8 and typically wear a size 8/10 or M/L. Currently, at 8 months pregnant I wear size M in Motherhood Maternity jeans and in lounge wear I size up to a L. I decided to order the 3XL of these pants and they fit me comfortably over or under my belly. But they will definitely be way too big postpartum. I plan to order the 2XL for postpartum. If you are a true 3XL you will be disappointed by the fit of these. The material of the pants is very thin & light. The color Black #2 has a bit of "sheen" to it almost looking like a sateen, but it doesn't feel like sateen-it feels like jersey knit.

Great pants in all sizes! Not see through! I love these pants! I bought them for my sisters and mother, a variety of sizes and body types. They fit all of us and I just bought the sizes I would regularly assume we wear. In the photo the sizes I bought and our measurements are listed from left to right; size small (she is 5’2 and 120 lbs), size xxl (5’8 and 260lbs), size medium (5’5 and 160), and me wearing a medium (5’4 and 145). One of us had a full pair of black pants underneath in this photo and at least one other was wearing black underwear and you can’t tell, which is awesome! They are long on all of us, and there is a large waist band, the second photo shows me with the waist pulled up all the way covering my belly button and meeting my bottom rib, I probably could have gone with a smaller size as they are quite loose and really stretchy but I’m happy all the same :) the fabric is cool, I can cuddle my dog and roll around with him and they don’t get hairy 🙏🏻.

Comfy, high-waisted, thin material - perfect house pants! I really love these pants. The material is thin and somewhat stretchy, and they are very comfortable! I'd be happy wearing these around the house any time of year (in California). I was looking for some comfortable lounge pants to wear at home and was delighted to find such cute ones! The high waist is fine, not my favorite, but I love the colors and the length is great on me. I'm 5'8" and about 145 lbs and I got a large, and they are a perfect fit. Now I can be lazy in style with these cute, comfy pants! I might just need some more pairs...Edit: Five years later I still wear and love these pants! I don't wear them every day but I wear them plenty and they have held up nicely. I have since had a baby and put on some weight and now weigh about 170 lbs, but I bought another pair in large last week and it's as comfy as ever! I love these pants so much!

Nice quality, but fit long as expected Purchased for lounging around the house or as pajama pants. They're very comfortable! The material feels soft, and feel thick enough that patterned or bright colored underwear might not be too noticeable through the white stripes. I bought a size small, I'm 5'4" w/ an average build and they are long for me as I expected. Although the waist is adjustable, the fit in the waist is fine, but I will have to fold the waist band over for a better fit. Even folded these are a couple inches too long for me. But I am pleased with the quality! I do wish an x-small size was available!! Purchased a second pair with a different design, just as happy!

Waist is good, long in length I'm a size 12/14 or XL usually in regular clothes. I ordered a size XXXL based on their chart and reviews. I like them and wear them plenty. I could have gotten away with a XXL, but I'm perfectly fine in a 3XL as well; they're not falling down or anything if I tie the string. I'm 5'7" and these are looooong. I can wear them with flats, but they have to be pulled up a bit high on my waist (they do have a decent length crotch too though so you don't get a camel toe when you do that lol) You can dress these up or down. I wear them just about everywhere. Pair with a sheer blouse for work or with a tank top for the store. Very silky and light enough for summer in the mid-south. I got 2 colors and will be buying more.

Comfortable and beautiful! I have been seeing pants like these for several months on Pinterest and they just seemed too good to be true. Cute, affordable, and comfy- could I really have stumbled upon the trifecta of pants? I did! I am 5’7” and as I’m on the curvier side and have long legs, I ordered the XL. They fit perfectly and the pattern looks just like the pictures. I know they’re intended to be more of a lounge pant, but the print is so beautiful I think I want to dress them up. With a fitted top and nice sandal these might be cute dresses up. They’re so light they’ll do well in warm or cool spring or summer weather. I hope they hold up. I’ll likely wash them in cold on the delicate cycle and line dry. If they survive their first few washes, I’ll be buying these for every woman I know.

Perfect fit, extremely soft, very thin Perfect fit, SO SOFT!! Bought the gray striped version in 3X (24 in the US). It loses a star only because it's very thin material that shows a bit too much. Also, I'm 5'3" and they brush the ground. I love everything just a little long so perfect length for me. That said, I've purchased other prints to try them out. 2 more patterns of this item: All are the same thin-but-soft material. -- Black stripe is just like the gray but immediately lost the string from the waist upon first wash. -- Navy floral same plus picture attached. I put my thumb straight through the seam on first wear after about 5 hours. I have another pair but have not worn them yet. They lost the drawstring in the wash as well (I even made sure to tie them before washing, I think the material is just too "slippery". Down to 3 stars now.

Love super long pants and arrived a day later Love these pants. Exactly what I wanted. I love super long pants. I ordered a 2x I'm usually an xL so I went up a size and they fit perfect. I'm 5 foot 3 and 185lbs. A lil long but I'm sure they'll shrink a little and I like my pants dragging on the floor lol. I ordered a black pair but didn't try them on yet. I normally don't like this material but its soft, comfortable and flattering. They ferl like jersey sheets and doesn't appear to be see through. Would buy again. Update, washed it and did not shrink but out of 2 pairs of these pants, the string came out of one of the pairs in the wash

Comfy and cute! Looks just like the picture and feels super soft! The material is good quality and an appropriate density. It isn't thin enough to be see-through, but it isn't so thick that it's hot. The pants do run large, though. I'm a 6 in pants and usually size up for PJ's, but the medium swallowed me. The small is still a little big, but fine for PJs. When sizing down from a medium to a small, the pants lost a few inches in length. I'm almost 5'8", and the medium was quite long (I loved it, but the length might not be for everyone). The small is a fine length for my height, though I wouldn't want them to be any shorter. Overall, great PJs, just go down a size!

Easy, Breezy, Comfy & Cool As a plus-size girly it is HARD to find palazzo pants that fit and also flow. But these are the ones! I love these pants so much that I bought two more pairs (chocolate & black) in the same size. I’ve worn them to church, date night, around the house, and running errands. They are as soft as your favorite sleep shirt but not see through. Flowy enough that you won’t get hot in the summer but don’t show panty lines. The ruching across the midsection is strong enough to pull high to natural waist or down around your hips and it’ll stay there. Some slight pilling after several washes but nothing big enough to be bothersome. (And my only other complaint is that, while your cell WILL fit in the pockets, excess weight WILL pull the waistband down a bit. Not all the way, just feels weird). If you are looking for breezy summer trousers, buy these. Edit: I am 5’8” and weigh 275lbs

Size DOWN at least 1 size! 5'2" - 165-170 lbs - 41"-36"-41" - White is lined! Hot weather approved. TLDR — Size down at least 1 size, if not 2. Length is good for some petites (5'1" to 5'3") and regular height (up to 5'7"). I'm 5'2" and weigh between 165-170 lbs. depending on the time of the month. My measurements are 41" Bust - 36" Waist - 41" Hips. I generally wear size 10-12 or L-XL depending on the brand, fabric, type of clothing, etc. I have a small FUPA but no butt, so occasionally I can size down to a size 6-8 or M (Medium) depending on the stretch of the fabric. I'm short-waisted and usually wear mid- or high-rise with a short or regular inseam depending on the pants and type of shoes I'm wearing. I ordered size L (Large) as suggested by Amazon in the color white, and it's too loose and baggy overall. The smocked waistband is comfortable and super stretchy. The white linen pants have lining inside all the way down except 6" from the bottom so it's not see through! You can't see the deep pockets... maybe very faintly if you're looking really hard in the picture. (I wish other white pants are lined like this, because there is nothing more annoying than having white pants that are practically transparent where you can see the pockets or even your nude underwear.) Because the pants are lined, it does add some noticeable weight to the overall feel of the pants, and along with the super stretchy smocked waistband, it causes them to slowly slide down. The pockets are deep and a great addition, and you could fit a phone in there, but because of the overall "heaviness" of the fabric and lining, the phone would definitely pull down the pants because the waistband won't support it. For this reason, I would size down to a size Medium for a relaxed fit or even a Small for a tighter fit. I'm only 5'2" and have the smocked waistband pulled above my belly button. The length of these pants are 41.5" with an inseam of 27.5". I'm wearing 2" platform sandals where the pants are almost grazing the floor. If you're shorter than 5'2" and don't want to wear 3" heels/ wedges/ platform sandals, these may be too long for you. Check the size chart for the overall length of the pants. According to the tag, this is made in China and it's only 30% linen and 70% rayon. It says, "Dry clean only. Do not bleach. Steam iron." I used a garment steamer to get the wrinkles out. The fabric isn't scratchy like typical linen since this isn't 100% linen. It felt light and breathable, even in hot, humid 92°F weather. Sitting on them obviously causes them to wrinkle in the back but the front remains mostly wrinkle free. Based on the size chart, I think going by the hip measurement would be best. Size medium for me would provide a much better fit where the waistband is hopefully a little tighter but the pants are still relaxed enough, while size small would provide an overall tighter fit. I'm debating on washing these to shrink them down a little as others have mentioned. Aside from the dry cleaning care instructions, who has time for that?! I do like these pants a lot and will order another pair or two in different colors and smaller sizes. I'll update my review later if I remember.

Perfect travel, dress-up/dress down pant. Better than shorts for a classier look. Only thing that helped me survive 95 degree temps in Europe this summer. So loose and breathable, I bought 9 colors! I was so comfortable and it kept the hot sun off my legs. I'm 5'1", 116 lbs. I hemmed them a bit and after washing them in cold and line drying them, they still want to shrink. Stretching them back into shape while wet seems to do the trick. In fact the only reason for four stars instead of five is that the care instructions are not clear that it should be dry clean only. So my recommendation is as if you need to hem them, wash them first and then see how much you need to take off after. If you're tall and they fit you perfectly out of the package then I would definitely just dry clean them.

They are a fancier looking version of my linen beach pants and these are not see through. Im very happy with these for the price.

Breezy linen with long legs These pants are a pleasant surprise! The linen is heavier than expected for a good drape and no show through; at least not in the khaki color. The styling is long and flowy, albeit longer than expected. In the attached photos, the pants haven’t been washed and dried yet, but they fall over my feet; even in platform flip flops. I’m just over 5’5” and ordered size XL. Taller wearers will likely be pleased with the length. The waistband is wide and stretchy, even just a touch snug. Since these pants are heavier gauge linen, I think I could get away with them this fall paired with boots and a sweater. Versatility is never a bad thing!

SUPER CUTE SWEATS! I ordered these ANRABESS Women Wide Leg Baggy Cargo Sweatpants in a size Small for my 10 year old daughter and she loves the way they fit! For reference, she's 5' tall and 105lbs-ish with a 26" waist. Though these pants are a little long on her, she insists that is fashion... and I'm here for it! Here's what we like about these sweatpants: •QUALITY GARMENT: These sweats are well made. The stitching is straight and secure. They feel more like a higher end pair of sweatpants than fast fashion. •SOFT: These sweatpants are made of very soft, cozy material. While they are warm, they aren't extremely thick or heavily lined with fleece. •FASHION FORWARD: The actual cut of these pants is really nice! They are very baggy in the legs, specifically at the knees. But they taper up at the waist, giving them a really good shape. My daughter loves the cargo pockets on the sides. •WASH & WEAR! These pants can be washed, dried, and worn dozens of times without compromising the quality. Mom win! Overall, my daughter is just obsessed with these! She likes to wear them with tank tops and a bunch of accessories (not shown in the picture, but she was just lounging). She also wears them with graphic tees and athletic shoes. I just like that they are affordable, comfortable ad made well. We highly recommend them!

LOVE THESE!!! I am 5'4 and 130lbs for reference and these are a small, I usually get my sweats a size or 2 up but these are a small and they are perfect. They do add some weight to your lower body as they are really wide legged, but they are so comfortable I'll over look it. They have side and cargo pockets and draw string for tightening them up a bit. If you are like me and wash your favorites sweats once and a great while these WILL create that " I'm wearing a diaper" look because they will sag off you. Good news is you can wash and dry them without shrinking or fading. They are long so they collect dirt AND they get wet when it rains outside.

nice and comfortable, super flowy! I ordered a large based on the size chart, and found the pants to fit well. I normally wear a size 10, and prefer my pants to have at least a 30 inch waist to fit comfortably. These were spot on and while medium would've been fine, the large gave me the nice "paper bag" pant look with the rouching at the top. I'm 5'5" and found the inseem really worked nicely without being too short (they're not as short as they appear on the model, unless maybe you hike them up more?). The fabric is medium weight, and is actually a bit ribbed with a pronounced seem in the front (not the back). They did come a bit wrinkled, so I'm not sure how they'll hold up when traveling, although that is what I got them for. They're so comfy though so I'll give it a try on my next flight and see what happens! They feel like my pajama pants, so will be ideal for overnight flights while looking chic. I got the dark khaki and they are actually a bit darker brown, and even a bit greenish than the photo shows. Overall I'm super happy, particularly for the price!

The good, the bad and the rest These pants are so soft on the outside, I keep finding myself running my hands over them. For reference I'm over 50 years old. Height 5'6", weight 170lbs measurements Bust: 43 Waist: 34 Hips: 43. I chose the XLarge in Navy Blue These pants remind me of the old velour track suits. As you can see in the picture, the ribbed nature of the fabric really isn't seen unless you're up close. And the blue is a deep color that unless the light is shining on them, they just read as dark. The fabric is fairly lightweight and does drape nicely. There is a fair bit of stretch to them, but I'm glad I went with the Xlarge just for the comfort of being baggy. The inside of the fabric is not as soft, but it is smooth and isn't aggravating to my sensitive skin. The stitching all seems fine, primarily just constructed on a serger. The elastic waistband is good, with a channel in the middle for a draw string. The pockets are nice and deep, I can even put my kindle Fire 7 in them. I would recommend washing inside out as they do pick up everything. My cat rubbed against my leg and it was covered. I'm not sure that I would wear them out as they really seem a bit too casual to me and given that they pick up lint easily, I'd have to carry a sticky roller with me. Washing inside out they didn't shrink, bleed color or change texture. Any wrinkles just give them a crushed velvet look and seem like it's meant to be that way. For that reason I can see traveling with them. Overall, they are very comfortable and soft and I can see myself chilling in the house wearing these often. Peace and comfy pants

Oh so soft and comfy From the description and pictures, I was expecting these to be like a ribbed tee-shirt knit, but they are actually like a crushed velvet. The fabric is lightweight and flowy, with enough stretch to be comfortable, and the waistband is wide and soft. I got the navy blue and, they looked m black in the picture I took because the light wasn't so great, but it's a beautiful dark navy. They also fit true to size. I'm 5'6" and they are maybe an inch longer in me than the model picture. It's not a problem for me, but if you're planning to wear these for more than lounge pants you should know that the fabric makes them look more like pajama pants than something I'd wear to work, on a date, or running errands like the description says. As for travel, I'm all about comfort and have no problem with a cute lounge wear set, but these not so much. Maybe on road trip, but I think they are too PJ-ish for a flight, for me anyway. I still love them, though, and will wear them around the house. They are also lightweight enough to easily pack for traveling as PJs or loungewear on days you aren't going out. A note on laundering: You do have to be careful about what you wash them with, because they pick up lint easily.

Pretty Style, Soft, and Easy to Wear Anyproud Wide Leg Pants are quite nice, as long as you like this style trousers. The material is soft and it's a good weight (not thin nor overly thick). The pants are not see through. The high waist design is attractive and the elastic waistband provides a good hold without being too restrictive or tight. The tie is honestly too long. I'll probably be cutting both sides to give it a reasonable length. The fabric is free flowing - the pants are in no way form-fitting or styled to accent your figure. I would say these are perfect for casual Sunday afternoons. They look just a tad too much like pajamas (in my opinion) to wear out to run errands or meet friends for coffee. That said, others may be quite comfortable in public wearing this pair. The fit is comfortable and aligns with their chart. At 5'7", the 'Small' hangs down to the top of my feet. I love the color, I love the flowing look, I love the feel of the fabric. This is a nice, good quality, casual pant that is well crafted and well designed.

Super Comfy! I am absolutely loving these sweater pants. They are wide legged, high waisted, stretchy, and comfortable as can be. They have an elastic waist with a drawstring and I would say they are true to size. I weigh 150 pounds at 5’7 and I ordered a medium. I do have longer legs, and these fall at the perfect length. There is a cable knot design that goes down the outside seam on both legs. The material feels like a sweater and is incredibly soft. I’ve worn these pants twice and have not washed them yet, but should they not wash well I will come back and update my review. What I like about them is they can be dressed up or down. They’re perfect For a party but also perfect for lounging. I like the diversity of occasions that I can wear these pants too. They look and feel like a quality pant, even after wearing them. Some pants stretch out weirdly, but these pants are not one of them. Very happy!

👠 Elongated Crotch - Runs True To Size For Reference I am ( 36C 162 lbs 5’4” size med / large ) Love the color on these khaki hue sweater pants, but what is most impressive is the wide range of sizes and lengths that is available. The size inclusivity for individuals who require a shorter inseam is Fantastic ! The only negative is that it is limited to only 2 colors ( black & brown ) I would love to see a wider range of color options. The sizing chart states the petite inseam as 26.8”. I measured mine to be a 27” inseam. The fabric is comprised of 50% Viscose, 28% Polyester and 22% Nylon. The material is soft and has a opaque appearance which falls right in between a knit and a sweater. It is not itchy but does offer a good amount of stretch and sight warmth. The elasticized waistband is 2” thick and offers a working drawstring for an adjustable fit. I did not find these to be a high rise as they sit right on the waist on me. What I did find is that the crotch area is elongated, which looks unflattering, but can be slightly corrected by rolling up the waistband as an option, which then will effect the length of the pants. The description refers to these as straight leg design, however the pants I received are straight just up to below the knee, it then flares out to a wide leg hem. What gives these pants some interest is the vertical outseam which runs the length of the pants from the waistband to the bottom hem. The seam in my opinion elevates the appearance of these pants so they can be utilized not only for casual or lounge purposes but with a pair of heels can be taken into the work place. Machine washable according to the care instruction label - cold delicate cycle but I found that hand washing has helped prevent shrinking due to the fabric makeup

Silky soft and warm knit pants with a bit of style. These straight leg, business / casual pants are quite comfortable. They are made of a super soft knit material that is a little fuzzy too. I got the X-Large Petite size and they fit well around my waist, but were a little shorter than I prefer on leg length. I am 5' 4", and often pants are too long. Since they had the petite sizes I went with that, but I guess I am borderline between sizes for the sizing of these pants. According to the chart and X-Large in regular would have 42.1" legs and I know those would be too long. Sort of a pickle on that one, lol. They are well made with solid stitching. The pleat down the front gives them a more stylish look for wearing away from home as well as lounging at home. They have a drawstring for waist adjustment, if needed. There are no pockets, though. With the thickness of the material, I thought they would be very warm, but the knit material must breath well as I wore them for 15 minutes or more while doing a couple things in the house and they were just as warm as the thin pants I had on before trying these on. They arrived in a zip bag and folded with them was a piece of tissue paper. Not sure why, but the tissue paper left tiny (pin-head sized) pieces of white here and there on the pants. The paper looked fine with no tears, but somehow it shed onto the pants, lol. Just mentioning as they were very visible on the black pair of pants I got. Over all, I'm recommending these knit pants. They are of a nice quality knit material and well made. Only caution I will say is study the size chart and hopefully the sizes will work better for the next person!

Comfy jeans I'm often hesitant to try jeans online but the sizing on these was thankfully alright. For reference, I am 5'5" and about 155 lbs right now. I got these in a size 8 which is what I typically try, but these days I might be a 6 to a 10 depending on the company selling the jeans. It's hard to trust jean companies anymore even when the give waist/length sizes, because pretty much nobody fits each parameter perfectly. Anyway these are very comfy and all pockets are open and functional. Gotta say I like the two forward pockets especially as most front jeans pockets you'd be hard pressed to actually put anything in. These actually fit my small phone in a pinch and do fit a wallet or other small object. The blue is nice and the pre wear on the pocket doesn't look like it will rip out too fast. It's tolerable unlike most pre wear/shredded types that you know are going to give you issues in cold weather or become functionally an issue. The flared bottoms aren't my typical style but they're nice. I've already gotten a few compliments on them. One potential addition for the company would be either a double button or a snap plus button. I'm a bit concerned the main button could eventually come out as its placement seems a bit tight on the waist. Otherwise though, I really like the feel of these.

Very Comfortable Jeans! These are a really comfortable pair of jeans. They are in the more current style, being on the shorter side and flared. I have to say I really like the fit. They have a good bit of stretch to them, so the size 18 fit me fine, and that's my usual size for most jeans. I like the color, which is a bit lighter, but it is still what I would consider a standard jean color. They are relatively high-waisted, but due to all the stretch, they are not going to "hold" you in the way jeans with stiff material will. I like the length on me, I'm 5'6 for reference, and they hit just at my ankle. My only pet peeve is that, basically, the entire pair of jeans is standard with no distressing, except for a random tiny spot on one of the back pockets. I think because this is the only spot for any distressing, it looks more like an actual tear or rip than intentional distressing. It's not a big deal, but that's the only thing I don't like about these jeans.

Super Comfortable Every girl needs a good pair of sweatpants and I had a pair that i’ve loved for 5+years— they’ve become very tattered and old so it was time for a new pair. Pleasantly surprised with the quality of this pair, they are very soft and cozy. I am 5’2 and roughly 145lbs curvy athletic build, and they have a little bit extra room but are cuffed at the ends so not super long. I think if you are a tall girl or are longer in the legs, these might be too short. Only negative thing i have to say is they are sooo high waisted on me. I do have a shorter torso but they could easily sit right below my breast line— simple fix is i just roll the waist band a bit— i’m not planning on wearing them outside of the house so aesthetically the rolled waistband isnt great but my primary use for these pants is just to lounge so i don’t care how it looks. Update day 2 of wearing these and they have started to pill on the inner thigh part of the pants— i haven’t washed them yet and it’s just from wearing them

High waisted and baggy! Yea.... I can see how these would make one feel like MC Hammer. These are definitely ultimate lounge pants. I measure 32-24-36 and ordered the xs which is my normal size. The waistband is indeed very tight, it only fits best way up on my waist. The crotch is super super long. Don't even bother rolling these bad boys down either because you will have created one huge spare tire around your hips. Big enough to keep people out of your personal bubble though, so maybe that's a good thing. The material is very thick and warm which I absolutely love because I am always cold. Its soft on the inside. The pockets are nice as usual. After seeing how big these pants were when I opened the package I though maybe I could use them for period week pants, but because the waistband is so tight, I don't honestly know if that would give me the relief I'd be looking for. These feel pretty sturdy though and as long as I don't gain any inches right below my ribs I should be able to wear them for a long time.

High-waisted at natural waist, baggy sweatpants, loose ankle cuffs, cute with crop top These are nice quality, thick material, well-stitched, heather grey sweatpants. This High-Waisted style fits right at my natural waist. The elastic waist is slightly snug on me, plus there is a drawstring cord. The rest of the pants are pretty baggy all over, including the butt, front/crotch, hips, legs, and loose ankle cuffs. There are 2 good pockets, one on each side, plenty roomy for hands or phone or keys. I usually wear size Small in sweatpants, size 4 in jeans. I have gained a few pounds, so I feel thicker around the middle than usual. But for reference, I am 5'7" tall, 130#, and ordered size Small. These sweatpants look better on the online model than me, and look cute with a tight crop top since they are high-waisted and baggy. But I think they are generally comfy, loose sweats. I think my teenage daughter will probably steal them from me! Thank you!

Odd fit These pants have a very strange fit. For context, I'm 5 feet tall and weigh 122 pounds, and I ordered a small. First, the pants have an extremely high waist; I have to pull them up to my chest for the crotch to fit properly. Additionally, the pants are really long, so they might work better for those with longer legs. The most unusual aspect of these pants is that because the material is thin, they are lined with a pair of shorts. Flowy not linen pants Light weight flowy fabric just what I was looking for. These are full length. I’m 5’8” and didn’t want them short; wearing a XL. They are not linen which I was also considering for summer, but this will not look sloppy and get overly wrinkled. Great for travel! Yes! Pockets!

Lovely fabric, quality garment, great fit, flattering These trousers were selected for a woman 5'8", 165 lbs., and the Large size fit is perfect. These slacks are lovely, dressy, the fabric substantial with a nice drape, the needle work perfect. This is a quality garment that should sell for a lot more than $30. Very flattering! The buttons are pretty, but only the waist button is functional. The buttons on the pocket flaps are for aesthetics only as are the flaps themselves, but there really are front pockets. The two back pockets are aesthetic only. The two cargo pockets are real. I like that the cargo pockets are placed lower on the leg and away from the hip, keeping the hips looking slim. The elastic waist in the back is well done and allows a good fit while allowing for roominess. This is a good thing because there are only two belt loops which are located on the front of the slacks so a belt is not really an option. This is the only thing that I find to complain about. Again, this is a quality garment suitable for dress or casual wear. I highly recommend you get them while you can at this price. I know I will be further investigating the AusLook brand for more quality bargains.

Placement of back pockets is flattering as well so yea, if you need cute dress pants (or slacks as my job calls them lol) grab a pair! I am 5'3 and they bunch a little at the bottom but it's really not a big deal. It's not amazons fault I stopped growing by 12 years old

PERFECT stretchy pants! LOVE these pants! These are EXACTLY what I was looking for. They’re stretchy, flattering and have REAL pockets, ladies. PERFECT for work and literally anything else since they’re so comfortable and flattering. I usually wear a medium, but took everyone’s advice from the comments and sized up to a large. For reference I’m about 5’5 and 136 pounds and usually wear an either 6 or 8. Don’t spend hours on amazon looking and preparing just get these.

Fits as expected, but not a lot of "stretch" I normally wear a large, and bought size large. The pants fit as expected, but they don't stretch a lot. So if you're at the high end of a large, you might want to go up a size. I'm 5'2". The pants are pretty long. For women under 5'5", they look like they will work with boots, but not with flats/other. They look like they will fit women up to about 5'7" nicely.

true to size, just ok I am a bit bummed about these pants. Lots of other reviews said they sized up and they fit a BIT large it wasn't a big deal because they had a drawstring. Well....I sized up and they are HUGE and the drawstring does NOT help. For reference, I am 5'7" and 140/145lbs, I usually wear a small or medium in bottoms and sized up to a medium. They are quite large on me. I plan to wash them to see if they will shrink down a bit, so stay tuned there. The drawstring is fully functional. The pockets are large and easily fit my iPhone 12. The material is a bit scratchy and doesn't have a lot of movement, I am hoping the washing will help with that as well. The material is a bit see through in the white. I had on darker panties when trying them on and they are very visible. However, because they are a looser fit, its nothing indecent. You can see the pockets through the fabric. Overall, I would go with your true size as they are meant to be oversized. The pants are just ok overall but hopefully will improve with a wash.

Mixed Feelings These pants are some how too big yet a bit snug at the waist. I appreciate that the expansion is on the side instead of the large band at the front. But that doesn't give much room or comfort at the front because the elastic is at the sides. Pre-pregnancy, I wore a US 6 to 8 in pants so I ordered the medium. They are quite big around the hip and leg area to the point that it looks like I'm wearing the wrong sized pants. The length also is a problem. I'm 5'4" and while I understand they're supposed to be a cropped fit, they just look like high water pants on me. They aren't flattering on me at all. They are, however, pretty well made pants. The denim is thick and sturdy. There are no front pockets because the elastic is where the pockets would be. They do have back pockets so that's good.

As expected from reviews, extra-flowy and soft, fits just right. Colors are true. I love these pants. Took them right out of the package and put them on. Okay, my review says "fit as expected." But that's because I read all of the other reviews and ordered a 2x. I am 5'6" and weigh about 180, with a bit of a tummy and some of that weight in the rear as well. Depending on the manufacturer and cut of garment, I can wear a Large, X-Large, or 2X. Around US size 16. About the color, and translucency, I ordered teal, and the color is perfect. The fabric is rayon, so it's very much like tightly-woven silk, actually, like sueded silk, and no more sheer than a silk blouse, for example. If I stand with the sun behind me, I am pretty sure you will vaguely see my legs silhouetted, but not the color of my underwear. Dark prints under light colors might show a little. The bottoms are hemmed and rest on the tops of my feet. A 1-inch heel will do the trick. UPDATE: I first saw them inside under incandescent light, which is very yellow, so they looked more teal. That is, a saturated greeny blue. In daylight, they look more blue. In the photo you can compare them to my t-shirt. In response to other reviews, I do plan to wear them to work. UPDATE: May 2017 I eventually got these pants in black, light green, and brown, in 1x, which are about a half-inch shorter. Otherwise I tend to step on the hem when going upstairs. The 1x waist still fits fine. The wide, wide legs are fun to walk in. They kind of flow and swing. They look great with tunics. Or a long vest, or one of the long, side-tail "cardigans" from Meaneor. The dye never ran, but I do wash the darks with other dark colors. I hang them to dry. I wore them a lot last summer and fall. I am updating my review because I am back for more!

So flattering!!! These jeans are gorgeous! And they have enough cotton in them to feel super soft, and enough stretch to feel comfortable sitting and walking in them. They are high waisted and have a nice flare that hits the shoe, just like I wanted it to (I’m 5’6”). They wash fine on medium but I let air dry because I don’t want to risk shrinkage in the dryer. I went with my “regular” size, but could have probably fit in the one below, but less comfortably. So I would suggest go with your regular size unless you are hesitating between two, and in that case go with the lower one. Also: the color is gorgeous!!!

Knee length shorts - for real! Finally, some knee length shorts! That is what I really like about them. So much so that I also got them in black and navy. These shorts remind me of the ones we wore back in the day, and I was happy to have found them. There are a lot of different shorts on this listing, so it can be confusing. This review is for the white shorts with the buffalo check print that you can fold up. From my experience, this style is the longest they carry. I found them to run a little loose, and there is elastic in the back. They sent a little belt with it, but I haven’t even taken it out if its package yet. I recommend following their size chart. These white shorts are not see through with nude underwear! I am including a photo of me wearing them today and I tucked the back of my HOT PINK shirt into them so you can see for yourselves. The one thing I personally dislike about these shorts is the brown buffalo check material that is sewn on. Thankfully I can wear it unfolded and not have that show. Unfortunately, it kinda does show through just a tiny bit, although my husband said he didn’t notice. Also it buttons where you fold it up, and the buttonhole is not done that well. I am letting these things slide since I am happy to have a decent pair of white shorts that are long enough. For reference, I am 5’9”, 134 lbs, and I bought size small.

Capris fit very comfortably, good for hot weather, though fabric could be better I got these capris because I needed some brown capris to wear this summer. SIZE & FIT: I ordered the capris in size XXL based on the size chart. That’s also my usual size. They fit quite comfortably (I am 5”6’, ~220 lbs, with very wide hips). The design of the capris is casual, so if you’re looking for a tighter fit and you’re on the cusp of two sizes, you may want to size down. The fabric has slight elasticity. The bottom hem falls just under my knees. FABRIC: As I said, the fabric is just slightly elastic. I’m not a big fan of the texture; it’s neither coarse nor soft, but rather somewhere in between. I could see this fabric being nice and breathable in the heat of summer, though. The fabric is also prone to wrinkling. These qualities of the fabric are the main reason I’m giving the pants 4 stars; I was hoping for something a little better for the price of $32.99 (at the time of this review). APPEARANCE: The capris look nice enough on me, except when they’re very wrinkled. I could see myself wearing them in business or casual settings. I like the shade of brown; it's a nice warm brown. BELT: The belt is weird because it doesn’t have any holes. It doesn’t offer much support, but then, it’s not very constricting, so it adds to the comfort of the pants. Overall, I’d recommend these pants in terms of their comfort, but maybe not so much in terms of the fabric.

Nice alternative to a skirt or shorts These capris are less high waisted than they are wide legged. I like the more modern, casual fit; comfortable without looking too frumpy. They are 95% cotton, which is rare these days. They did not shrink after washing in cold and hanging to dry. Although I am purposely choosing to roll with the slightly wrinkled look for a more causal vibe. This look is ideal for warm weather when a skirt or shorts could be warn without the concern of showing your legs. Yes please. I ordered a small, although I usually wear a medium or 6 in pants. There really isn't a need for a belt, but it does jazz up the overall look and was a thoughtful accompaniment. The belt is brown 'pleather' and looks surprisingly decent. But with the elastic waist band in the back, a belt really isn't necessary. There are belt loops that run around the entire waistband. I like the simple styling of the two front pockets. but feel the cut in the back makes my rear look a little pancake-like and baggy. I have short legs and am 5'7", 138. The size small is quite ample. Anyone smaller would for sure want an XS, which I do not believe is available. These capris will be a welcome addition to my spring/summer wardrobe. I love a good neutral color and appreciate the simple style, comfort and the modern, wide leg-look.

I would say these are a must purchase for any party!!

Roomy, Comfy, Outstanding Pants!! These are very nice lounge pants, they come in an array of colors, and I am extremely pleased with them!! I will definitely end up buying more colors.- The material feels sturdy & well-made, and it is fairly thick, so these pants are a little warm for summer, but they will make great lounge pants for wearing out during spring or fall or lounging around the house any time of year.- The legs are very wide, making them incredibly roomy & forgiving of any less than tone areas- No give in the legs (not that you need it), but there is plenty of stretch in the waist and I don't even need the drawstring to help keep them up- Fits is true to size. I normally wear smalls in everything (US 4-6), and the small fits me perfectly. For reference, I am 5'3", 125 lbs, 33" bust, 28" waist, 36" hips, size 3 in pantsI normally need a petite inseam (29"), so these are a little long for my short legs, but if I tug them up a bit, they don't drag the ground & get stepped on as I walk. In the photos, I have them pulled up to my belly button- I love that these pants also have pockets! They are not deep pockets, but they are enough to hold my iPhone 12, car keys, etc. and pockets are always quite convenient- The item description says, "Dry Clean", but the tag says to wash in cold water on delicate, tumble dry low, remove promptly. I have been washing them on regular & everything seems great so far. The material is definitely not one that feels like it requires a delicate cycle.The tag also indicates that they can be tumble dried on low heat, but I decided to air dry due to this conflicting information (and if any damage occurs, it usually happens in the dryer). The material is a little stubborn & likes to hold those wrinkles, so you will need to do a little steaming or ironing, but I think this is a small price to pay for such cozy pants!I think you could dress these pants up & wear them to work or out on a date, but they make incredible lounge pants too. Most - if not all - of my pants are either one or the other, so this is a very unique quality! I am extremely pleased with these pants, and I enthusiastically recommend them!!

In the photos, I have them pulled up to my belly button- I love that these pants also have pockets! They are not deep pockets, but they are enough to hold my iPhone 12, car keys, etc. and pockets are always quite convenient- The item description says, "Dry Clean", but the tag says to wash in cold water on delicate, tumble dry low, remove promptly. I have been washing them on regular & everything seems great so far. The material is definitely not one that feels like it requires a delicate cycle.The tag also indicates that they can be tumble dried on low heat, but I decided to air dry due to this conflicting information (and if any damage occurs, it usually happens in the dryer). The material is a little stubborn & likes to hold those wrinkles, so you will need to do a little steaming or ironing, but I think this is a small price to pay for such cozy pants!I think you could dress these pants up & wear them to work or out on a date, but they make incredible lounge pants too. Most - if not all - of my pants are either one or the other, so this is a very unique quality! I am extremely pleased with these pants, and I enthusiastically recommend them!!

Linen fabric is very nice, color is rich and drapes nice even though it’s a thicker linen. Recommend if you aren’t too tall or have a thicker waist.

Will shrink if dried in dryer! So first off, I’m only 5’4 and almost 150 lbs. These were perfect until I washed them with my regular laundry. To be honest I have never had clothes shrink to be too short so I wasn’t being careful. They now barely skim my shoes but do get a little longer if I wear them for a bit and stretch the fabric out. I certainly love them otherwise. The pockets, the fit and the color are perfect. Probably not for tall girls, just be warned.

Love these pants!! I love these pants and receive so many compliments while wearing them. They are comfortable and not too long for a short person (I’m 5’3”). They washed well and didn’t shrink. If you aren’t sure, go for it!!

Great fit! I hardly ever write reviews, but thought I would because I love these pants! I am a pear shape, and have a hard time finding flowy pants to fit my larger hips and smaller waist. These fit my curves so well, but also fit my waist in a flattering way. The material is light and flowy too. I hate wearing shorts in the summer, so these are a great fun option! For reference, I am 5’7” and about 185 pounds. I ordered an XL.

Fun & Frilly Pants! The - Blooming Jelly Women Linen Pants Summer Vacation Outfits Wide Leg High Waisted Pants Palazzo Flowy Pants Trouser 2024 – are fun and frilly pants that make me happy when wearing them. I love the way the fabric feels on my skin and moves when I walk. They’re not the most flattering style of pants for my fuller figure but I don’t care - I like them. The only somewhat negative comment I have is the sizing chart lists the measurements of the pants themselves and not the body. And they don’t seem to be that accurate in my opinion. I ordered the size XL which according to the chart the hip measurement is 42”. When I measured the pants I received while flat, I measured 52 inches at the hip. The X-Large does fit me but if I would like the pants to look on me as they do on the model, more roomy and gathered like a skirt under the elastic yoke area, then I should have ordered a larger size or two, if it were available. Regardless of all that, these pants are lovely and I’m very glad I chose to purchase them. For sizing reference, my Bust Measures 44”, my waist measures 37”, and my hips measure 47”. I’m 5’4” tall. I purchased a size XL.

These pants are just as cute as depicted in the description photos. The material is a thin but opaque, lightweight fabric with a little texture that drapes beautifully and doesn't wrinkle easily. They are NOT linen, as some of the lines in the item description suggest. The bow in front is sewn to either side, and is not a drawstring, while the rest of the waist is smocked and very stretchy. As for sizing, I am 5 foot 7, weigh 170 pounds, have a 36C bust with narrow shoulders, a short torso, and a wide but flat derriere, carry most of my weight in the middle, and wear a US women's size 10 or 30 pants (medium size pants and joggers at Old Navy and H&M), or a woman's 10 or 12 in dresses. I ordered these pants in the "large" size, which I think was the right fit for me (see my pics). While I don't think these are the most flattering pants for my apple shaped flabby stomach, they are super comfortable to wear with no pinching at the waist, and I can see myself wearing them often as soon as the weather warms up. Overall, these are cute, comfortable summer pants, and I'm happy with the quality. I would order from this brand again.

Cute and comfy! I love these pants!!! I ordered them for a trip to Amsterdam next month and I wanted pants other than jeans. I figured I'd give these a try because they seemed to get really great reviews and I found a pattern I love so why not give them a shot. When opened the package, the leg part looked a bit big and I thought they would look like Aladdin pants (LOL) but they don't seem so big when they're actually on. They're roomy but not too big and the material is light but comfy and soft-ish.I love how these come in a variety of prints/colors and are super easy to coordinate an outfit! I plan to wear these with a black tank or black tee and sandals (like I'm wearing in the photo) but I could totally see myself wearing a pair of flats or even heels to dress them up if I wanted to. I also think these would be comfy enough to wear all day while I'm sight-seeing, visiting museums, strolling around town, or going to a flea market.I typically dress in darker clothes but wanted something different, something funky for my trip to Amsterdam. I love bohemian, though I never thought I'd actually like these so much! It might be hard to tell in my photo but they are a perfect dark blue and black floral/mandala type pattern (the color is actually called "Mandala Blue"). The pattern looks exactly how I thought it would be (or is even better!) in person!For reference, I am 5'9" and about 180 pounds. The length of the pants is just right, a little above my ankles. Not too long and not too short even for a taller person. My only beef with these is that I think they look better high waisted however doing so gives me a little bit of camel toe which isn't ideal so I have to pull them down a bit more to my hips for them to be more comfortable (or maybe with a different tee or tank they could work better for me high waisted). I wish the waist part was a bit stretchier or longer, which is the only reason why I gave them 4 stars, but this is definitely not a deal breaker. I was more concerned about these being long enough for my liking and roomy in the leg/thigh area which they very much are.Aside from the high waisted fit thing (which is probably more on my body than the pants themselves), I am absolutely obsessed with these! I didn't expect to love them so much but I really do. They are effortless, super cute and versatile! I can't wait to wear these! I'm so glad I took a chance and tried something I might not have if it weren't for my trip. I will definitely be wearing these into the summer well after my trip to Europe! Thanks so much for a great item!Update: washed these once and I am noticing a few lines on these where the print has faded or the black bled through the fabric? Not sure whether I'm explaining right but they are definitely not wet spots from water as they're a straight line. I did not dry these in the dryer, I let them hang up to dry so I'm not sure why there is a blemish in the fabric. My OCD is killing me knowing these spots exist, but I don't think they would be noticeable to anyone else and I still love these pants.

These are my new favorite pants and as soon as I am done writing this review I am promptly ordering more in different colors. Easy to dress up or lounge around in. Fabric is perfect thickness for summer but it does wrinkle easy; nothing a trip through the dryer won’t fix. Says handwash (who has time for that?)but I threw it in a cold wash with the rest of my clothes and they fared well. I am 5’5” and 190lbs (and losing) and the large (10-16) fit perfect and I think they will still fit well as I lose more weight.

Diggin' my I Dream of Jeannie Pants! I just got them, so too soon to tell for the long term, but right off the bat: the material and fit is super cute, comfortable, and flattering. (For reference- I tend to be a size 8/medium and am about 5'4" tall and I got the 2-10 size) The material does seem a bit cheap so I can see how they would tear like in other reviews. Good thing I am not a gymnast or flexible because I could see them tearing right open if you did the splits. Would buy again if they keep the price low/affordable since they are obviously not meant for long term use. Will likely launder in a bag and on the gentle cycle in attempt to keep them in tact for awhile (fingers crossed!) Update: even being very careful with washing- got a tear right after the first use. Such a bummer since they seemed great otherwise. Would only buy again if priced under $20. Wouldn't pay more since you cannot guarantee more than one use.

great fit, but unfortunate pattern alignment I was given a pair of the ' Mandala Blue' harem pants pattern almost four years ago. I am 5'5" and about 130 lbs, and these are a large. They've washed well, gone through the washer and dryer no problem, and are very comfortable. All of the seams and elastic have held up.I am pleased enough that I am planning to buy another pair, in a different pattern, because ever since I realized the pattern lineup formed an unfortunate target on my backside, I haven't been able to wear them in public. I can turn them around, but having the target on the front isn't much better. Consider your pattern selection carefully if this concerns you!Update: after writing this I bought two more pairs of the solid colors. When they arrived I was upset, because they were stiff, huge, and looked like plastic. Fortunately they shrank significantly when I washed them, and lost the aggressive shine. Now they look and feel more like cotton/linen, and I don't feel like I'm in a 90's music video. Rating stays the same.

Woven Material Means No Stretch I usually would wear a 12 in modern women’s pants sizes because everything has some stretch to it. Keep in mind these pants do not stretch at all. I am 5’1”, 176 lbs with the weight in the lower half and I am pretty much at capacity for the seat/inseam (not that the seams are stressed, more that the pants will start to crease strangely in the front - if I could do the splits, I’d be able to do them easily and without an incident). I don’t plan to exchange because they’d be too long and the legs look good. I ordered black and they are thin but not transparent (you won’t see the color of your underwear). Like others have said, weight in the pockets (e.g. my iPhone 12 mini) will pull them down. Overall, I’d recommend giving them a shot.

For curvy girls - these only need minor adjustment for great fit Due to my shape, I struggle a great deal to find pants that fit. Usually my only options are granny styles or looks that are too risque for work. I checked the size chart and ordered a 12 to fit my hips, knowing it would be big in the waist. Of course, the stats help when reading reviews, so: 29.5" waist and 45" hips, 5'5" tall and 160 lbs. You can see in the first photo how loose the waist was. A quick adjustment with my sewing machine fixed the fit, and overall I'm pleased with how these pants look. Eventually I may have a go at letting down the hem to get a little more length out of them. For us ladies with significant curves, these pants require very minimal tailoring to achieve a lovely fit. At this price point, I'm thrilled to get such a professional look. One star off because they're extremely plastic feeling inside (though not outside). Overall, I'd highly recommend for curvy girls with basic sewing skills.

Soft and cozy but size up I love these pajamas! They are cute, cozy, and warm. I’m typically a women’s small (5’6” 130Ibs) but I like my pajamas a little loose so I ordered a medium and large. I ordered from the same seller and they even arrived in the same bag but they were made with different fabric and were wildly different in size. The large pants fit me like a glove and the top was loose but not overly so. I pulled the pants up because I like a high waisted fit and they still sit at my ankles. The fabric is stretchy and very plush. I was surprised they looked so similar to the stock photos. The buttons are on tight and there were only a few loose threads. I wish the arms were a little longer so the elastic could sit at my wrist instead of my arm but it’s still comfy. The inside of the pockets is a little scratchy and I’d prefer fabric covering the elastic on the ankle and wrist ruffles. Overall, the large pajamas were 5 stars. The medium pajama pants were too small to get on and too short. The top fit fine but the fabric on both is rougher, cheaper and less plush feeling. I would not have kept the medium even if they weren’t too small.

Perfect pants for weight fluctuations. I was injured in a car accident and gained quite a bit of weight. With that being said, I need to order new pants that would fit my new sizes. I saw these and remembered I had similar pants of a smaller size. I’m a fan of sticking to what I know. I am 5’6” tall. My waist is 31 inches and my hip is 44 inches. According to the size chart, I needed a large. The pants fit true to size. The pants themselves feel like really good quality. They’re lightweight but the material is still heavy. It’s not flimsy or see-through. These pants feel super durable. They’re comfortable yet still give a very professional appearance due to the style and design. Most people hate the scrunchie waist but I love it because it gives room for me to lose weight without having to have the pants tailored. The length of the pants is regular length, not long or short. My one complaint is the buttons. They’re hideous. That’s not a major issue for me because I can just change them.

Comodísimo - Fresco (un poco largo-5’2”) Debo decir que este pantalón ha superado mis expectativas (tanto que ya pedí otro color). Es increíblemente versátil, ideal para usar durante todo el día, en el trabajo, y también para salidas nocturnas. La tela es ligera y transpirable (aunque está forrada) perfecta para los días calurosos, pero también se ve lo suficientemente elegante para un entorno profesional. Lo que más me gusta es su comodidad; el ajuste es perfecto, permitiendo libertad de movimiento sin perder el estilo. Además, el diseño es moderno y atemporal, lo que facilita combinarlo con diferentes prendas y accesorios. Este pantalón se ha convertido en una pieza esencial en mi guardarropa. Es práctico, cómodo y adecuado para cualquier ocasión. Lo recomiendo encarecidamente a quienes buscan una prenda versátil y de alta calidad. Tomen en cuenta que mido 5’2 y me queda un poco largo-nada que unas sandalias altas no puedan resolver.

Super cute wide leg pants These pants are super cute pants and true to size (I'm a size 4 and ordered a small). The color pictured here is the black. They have an elastic waist with ties which is for those that usually need tightening at the waist. I like that they have pockets on each side that are pretty deep. The legs are super wide (bigger than bell bottoms) and flowy and the perfect long length (I'm 5'6" and the bottoms land right above the floor). I would suggest wearing these with a tighter top to offset the width of the pants, unless you are going for super casual look. The material is a bit thin (not see throughy) so these are better suited for warmer environment. The only down side I had so far was these are a bit static, I guess because they are polyester, so I need to find a way to keep them from sticking to my body as I walked (see picture three around the top). I could definitely see myself wearing these pants in white or a fun pattern at a beachy place. Would be perfect for that!

Really nice, but tight waistband I'm 5'5" and 115 lbs but with long legs and a preference for pants that almost touch the ground, so I have a hard time finding pants long enough. These are a small in the 33" inseam. They fit well although I wish they were an inch longer. I'm debating getting a pair in 35" and see if they work, but that might be too long. It's nice to have side pockets but if you have a big phone (like S22 ultra) the top 1.5 or 2 inches of your phone will poke out. My only real complaint is the elastic that runs along the very top of the waistband. I have a 27" waist and it really digs into me - enough that it's kindof uncomfortable. As embarrassing as it is to show my belly in the pics, I feel like it's necessary to show the fit. Definitely not flattering on the belly, at least not if you've had kids! But for day to day, they look fine bc I don't walk around in belly shirts. The uncomfortable waistband is the only thing keeping me from getting another pair. They feel well made and the fabric is really nice - nice and thick material, very soft and very stretchy.

Heavyweight, High Quality, Super Comfy, Structured-looking Sweatpants with Multiple Useable Pockets! These are high-end, nice looking, stylish sweatpants, not like the wrinkly, sloppy looking types at all. They look nice enough to go somewhere in without being embarrassed to be seen. They're made of a thick, heavyweight material with some body to it. They feel cozy to wear and will definitely keep me warm on winter days. The pockets are fabulous...deep and functional! My giant cell phone will actually fit inside one of the leg flap pockets! I ordered size Medium in Black and they fit me perfectly, except that they're just a tad long (before having laundered them). For reference, I'm 5'4", long-waisted (therefore proportionally somewhat short-legged), and about 124 lbs with a 29" waist and 38" hips.*UPDATE on gradual SHRINKAGE of the LENGTH of the INSEAM & RISE: I thought it might be to that after having worn and washed & dried these sweatpants a lot over the past couple of months...they've shrunk in length a bit! I suppose that's due to the cotton in their fabric content. So, they're no longer too long for me! The length of the rise has also shrunk some, so they still are high waisted, but they fit/look a bit better in that area as well.

Super super cute - midweight. Love the look. The pockets are placed perfectly - I have larger hips and they look amazing. I feel very trendy. I wear a 16 and got an XXL and I am 5’9” and they are long enough. I haven’t washed yet but even if they shrink they will be long enough.

Size up if you are going to machine dry I like the style of these mid rise jeans and they are flattering on my hourglass figure. I usually wear a womens 10 in pants and ordered a large based on the size chart. The fit me perfectly when I tried them on; however, they shank when I washed them. I washed these jeans in cold water and tumble dried them on a low temperature setting (the tag recommends hand washing and line drying, but who has time for that?). The length is still ok on me after drying them. I'm 5'6" and the length is good to wear with sneakers or flats, but the jeans would probably be a little too short to wear with heels. After washing, the stomach area is now a little tight on me. I could still wear them if it wasn't for the fact that the light weight denim fabric pulls out of shape at the zipper. I know these are mid-rise jeans, but the rise feels a little short. With the light weight fabric and the rise, there is a very subtle camel toe situation going on for me. There must have been an adhesive label on the back side of the waistband that I didn't notice before I washed them because there is now a spot of gummed adhesive that will probably never come out. The style of the pants is cute and the quality is fine, though they seem a little over priced at $39.99. If they hadn't shrunk, I would have been happy with them. If you plan to tumble dry these and wash in any temperature other than cold I would recommend sizing up at least a size.

Good for the price I got this pair first, and then also the white one. First you see what they look like with straight bottom. They become longer after being ironed, out of the package. I'm 5'10", so it is an acceptable length. However, It's still a little short for me, when I walk and move around and I found them not very flattering. I had this idea. Purchased a pair of shoelaces in similar color; cut a small hole on the 'wrong' side of the bottom of each pant leg so that it is between the very bottom and the horizontal seam, and then I threaded a shoelace in each, to tie them around the ankle. And these pants become what you see on the last picture, with a tie on the outer side of the leg. This style is more attractive to me, and works better with this garment for my height. I did the same with the white pair. And I wear both frequently. I haven't had a chance to wash them yet, since I wear them gently and mostly for walks. I heard from other reviews that these pants shrink and pill. Shrinkage is the bigger worry. I have other linen pants that shrink and stretch with washes and wear. My plan with THESE pants is to iron them while wet after the wash, and stretch them out, then hang-dry. In my experience, this works with clothes that are prone to shrinking. At least for a while. For pilling/fuzz, I keep a fabric-shaving machine. It's been a great help for many years, and there's a great selection of them right here on Amazon. Hope this helps.
