Attractive belt but concerned about longevity of material-Part leather part PU-brown side is dark I am around 120 pounds and 5'6 and usually wear 0-2. I ordered a size small and there are several holes still available and it fits great. However, I am concerned about the longevity of the material. It is not as high quality as I expect from Calvin Klein. This is made of split leather and PU leather. In concept, the PU leather coating would make the split leather a little more durable. However, the belt began to show signs of wear after trying it on. The metal bracket of the belt is coated and I can see the joining seams. It still has a nice appearance. The brown side is very dark and almost black in appearance. I am not confident in the longevity of this belt. I still like the belt but have not had it long and hesitate to recommend it fully.

I ordered a size small and there are several holes still available and it fits great. However, I am concerned about the longevity of the material. It is not as high quality as I expect from Calvin Klein. This is made of split leather and PU leather. In concept, the PU leather coating would make the split leather a little more durable. However, the belt began to show signs of wear after trying it on. The metal bracket of the belt is coated and I can see the joining seams. It still has a nice appearance. The brown side is very dark and almost black in appearance. I am not confident in the longevity of this belt. I still like the belt but have not had it long and hesitate to recommend it fully.

Super great belt! Love the two sided reversible concept, especially being done the white and black colors. White and black go with everything so there is no limitation. I love the fun twist of having the one side designed in the CK initials too. The buckle is a decently heavy weight but not so heavy that it weighs down your bottoms no pulls The waistline forward. The finish is a beautiful shiny silver. I would says this belt runs true to size. I selected a size large in the Black/White color option. For size reference I run 170-180 pounds and stand at 5'4". When I wear high waisted bottoms it fits my waistline on the smallest hole selection. Then when wearing low rise bottoms I have several hole size adjustments remaining that I can choose, which the belt fits perfectly each time. This as well fits my daughter who is of the same height but is 190-200 pounds. Being this is versatile for various bottoms, reversible, matches all outfit colors, and compatible with both my weight as well as my daughter’s I definitely recommend this belt!

Pretty standard belt I am normally a size 12/14 and went with the Large, but I should have gotten an XL. Around my waist, the belt is at the last notch and around my upper body (think, sinching at the waist dresses, etc), it fits me better. I guess that’s good for the most part as I won’t have any “extra overhang” of the belt. (But, of course, that means I can’t gain any weight either, haha). For reference, I am 5’4, 185lbs, and apple shaped. Should have definitely sized up. I love that the belt is reversable. I did notice the white size has a CK design stamped across it, but it’s not terrible as you can’t really see it and it just makes the white side look textured. The material is durable, and I think this belt will hold up for a long time.

Tall Girls Dream! Will stretch. Update: have owned for a couple of months now and they def do stretch out. I got the 8 and they were a touch whisker like on the crotch area but after a couple of wears, they stretch out to fit perfect, just like my navy ones did.i already owned these slacks in Navy that I purchased at Lohmans a bit ago. I'm 5'10 and a size 8. And the navy ones fit perfect and needed no tailoring. My old black slacks got lost so I purchased these in black and I hoped they'd fit like the Navy ones. If you are used to teen type sizes like H&M or Forever 21 than yes go down a size but if you are used to woman's brands like CK, Kenneth Cole, or Jones New York, than stay to your true size. At first I was nervous because they were a little tighter in the thighs than my navy ones but I wore them around the house for a day and they stretched to fit perfectly. These slacks do have lots of stretch which is great for comfort but know that they will give slightly once you're wearing them so a little tight when you first wear them is good. Otherwise they'll be baggy and nobody wants saggy slacks butt. They are long. The cut is perfect for women with a little hip. Not boot cut but wide enough at the bottom to give you a little balance. You can see in my photo that at 5'10 they go all the way to floor and at 39in hips fit pretty perfect. There's a little pull in photo from way I was standing but that's it. Good luck!

I think these would look better on someone who wore a medium or small as opposed to someone who wears larger sizes like me.

Fashionable denim cargo pants I really enjoy these cargo pants. They have a very sturdy feel without being bulky or heavy. I love that I can put my wallet and phone in both pockets and I don't feel like the pants are being dragged down. The color is a good medium without being too bright but not faded. I do wash this on delicate with WooLite to prevent fading/shrinking though. In terms of the fit, I rather like it. I am 5' 2", 170 lbs. I definitely carry most of my weight in the middle. These cargo pants can be worn perfectly high-waisted, but also don't hug my lower belly too much that it's unflattering.

Insufficient quality to last long I ordered these pants in "Green" and the pants I received are far less a true green than the appear on screen - more a muddy color, like a khaki green. That's fine - even preferred - for me, but not what I would've anticipated based on the image. They're attractive and fit well (I ordered based on the size chart). I'm 5'5" and they hit me at the ankle as they do on the model. I would probably not recommend these pants if you are taller than 5'6". I want to rate these well - these pants are listed at less than $20, so I take quality with a grain of salt. But the zipper on the pair I received is truly awful quality. I'll be surprised if it lasts a month before becoming unusable. No one else so far has mentioned the zipper quality, so if you want to take a chance, go for it. But unfortunately, I can't recommend these.

You can trust the size chart in the photos. I am 5’6”, 123 lbs, and normally a size 4 in pants. I chose the medium based off the waist measurement on the size chart and I’m glad I did. The waist has elastic so there is some give if you are between sizes. The material is not stretchy so I would recommend double-checking the hip measurements before ordering. The legs are wide and full length on me. I ordered the “khaki” which is actually very light yellow with no tan tones. The material is thin but not as revealing as expected during wear. I would prefer a darker color so I wouldn’t have to worry about under things as much. My review photos were taken after my initial try-on after removing the pants from the jar. I had not washed nor ironed but wanted to get pictures posted to show the thinness of the khaki aka light yellow. I like the fit and feel of these cotton summer pants. It’s nice to have a loose and breathable material for the Texas summers. Like most linen pants, these wrinkle easily. The color is my biggest gripe but that’s personal preference.

Kind of a high water look? I think paired with some wedges they'll look better on. But otherwise the fit is what I hoped for and is comfortable. Would recommend.

A LITTLE HIGH IN THE CROTCH, BUT NICE! I was looking for a wide-legged pant to go with my top. I purchased these in Orange and Red in sizes L, XL and XXL. I ended up going with the Orange pants in XL. At the time, I was about 210 lbs and I am 5'5".These are nice pants. They are a bit thin, so when I wore them, I put on a boy short underneath to smooth out the look. The crotch area is a bit high for my taste. So, even though they fit great, the crotch area can look a bit tight, giving the perception that they are too small, which was not the case. With a slight heel, I was great to go without having to hem them.I felt the Orange color 'popped' better over the Red, but both are nice colors.

Comfy…but for work. Size reference: I am 5’6” and 127 lbs. I normally wear a size 4 in pants and jeans. I ordered these pants in size small. The fit is true to size in the waist. The back is elastic so there is a little give if you are between sizes. When standing, the pants are full length but when I sit the cuffs come up to mid-shin. Description: I love the design with the pleating. I can get by with pairing with heels to wear to work because the ankles don’t have the bunched elastic jogger look. I also like the real button, zipper fly, and belt loops for the same reason. The outside material is soft, like yoga pants. The inside is lined with slick polyester. There is some stretch but not like true sweat pants. I do worry that the pants will snag easily because of the material. Also, I hope the rayon doesn’t shrink if I accidentally dry these. Recommendation: I love these pants. They are comfy like loungewear but dressed up enough for business casual. I ordered the green and there is some heathering in the background. If not for that, I would probably go ahead and order the black but heathered black looks too faded to pass as work pants. This is the 8th pair of pants I’ve gotten lately and by far the most comfy! I highly recommend!!!!

I Like These Pants I've been looking for pants like these for quite awhile. I wanted lightweight pull-on pants with pockets big enough for a cell phone that I can wear during the summer months. For me, the hardest part was determining the right size. The online size chart indicated that I should wear a size 18P, which is larger than anything I own. Nevertheless, I bought a pair and they turned out to be too big. I knew as soon as I tried them on, though, that I wanted one in every color. So, I ordered two more pairs in both a 16P and a 14P. The 16P turned out to be the best fit, even though it is still a little loose; but I definitely didn't want pants that were too tight. These are good every-day pants for a retired woman in her 70's. I am 5'2" and weigh about 160. I had no problems with making multiple returns.

Super soft but has some drawbacks These are really soft, priced reasonably, and very comfortable. Good points: - sizing is pretty accurate for the chart (I'm usually a L and the chart suggested XXL which was good) - color is beautiful - fabric is lightweight and super soft but it does show every dimple and buldge - The expenditure is totally on the mark for the value. Draw backs: - the waist is so high! A good 4" above my belly button. - the top seam is tightened with elastic so you can slide it down your waist without it creeping up - it has these faux pockets, so they just add extra bulk to the belly area and serve no purpose - it came wrapped in not one but three single use plastic bags for no good reason - it has a strong chemical smell, likely due to be wrapped up in so much plastic that needs a few times washing to get out - they are little long for my 5' 5" self so I have to hem them by a good inch or so Overall, these are cute and very cozy. I love the color too. I'd recommend them but be aware of the above issues before you buy.

Might be ok if you’re 5’5 or under If I had the right body type for these pants, I wouldn’t hesitate to give them 5 stars because they are really well made, the fabric is heavy and looks expressive. Unfortunately they don’t work for me. First of all, the length - I’m 5’7 and these pants are just way too short. They don’t work as ankle pants because of the wide leg, and I would need at least 3 extra inches of length in order to be able to wear them. Sizing- I got a Large for my 33” waist, 43” hip. You can tell the size is not right for me by the way they balloon on the front in the stomach area. I should have gotten a Medium. Keep in mind that the waist has an elastic so the waist stretches a good bit and the fabric itself is stretchy so my hips definitely would have been ok in a Medium. My suggestion is that if you’re 5’5 or under, give these pants a chance and if you’re in between two sizes, go with the smaller size.

Heavy fabric I chose Beige in these trousers which was on point. So glad since it goes with the neutrals in my closet. The hemline was agreeable for my 5’1” stature. I wore these belted a smidgeon over my belly button. This was the only way I could wear them with tennis shoes.A change in heels will dress these up. I must admit, these pants did ‘poof’ out due to the weight of the fabric I think. If you pair them with a cropped top, form-fitting tee or tucked in a thin sweater, doing so would offset the heaviness. These pants may not suit every shape and size so read as many reviews before buying.

Great Linen-like Material The material of this outfit was linen-like and had a good weight to it. The fit for me was a bit off. I am usually a large bottom and medium top. The bottoms were a bit tighter than expected and the top vest was too big and boxy. The pants are high waisted and wide leg. The material was stiffer so flattering and didn't cling. There was a zipper and elastic in the waistband so they could be put on easily. For me I would need to alter the vest for it to look better on but I could wear the pants with another top. The pants were a great length on my 5'4" frame when worn higher on my waist. The pants hit right above the floor so can be worn with flat sandals.

Perfect fit These pants are great. There's a lining like shorts inside and it doesn't ride up. You notice it a little bit when you're walking, like the hem of the shorts, but it's not very noticeable. It might be more visible if you're pretty dark skinned, though. They're flowy and the wrinkles steamed out easily--however, they wrinkle when you're sitting for a bit (I was at my desk half of the day) and those new wrinkles didn't go away when you get up. Not a deal breaker for me, but worth noting. I love that the waist fit me perfectly. There's no elastic, no stretch, and I used the measurements in the at size chart. These will prob be too long if you're short. I'm 5'8" and had on 2.5" heels and they were just above the floor maybe an inch. For size reference, I ordered XL and it's perfect. I'm 5'8", 173#, about a size 10. My mid waist is 34 and my hips are 40 inches.

Cute pants with some flaws I was really excited for these pants and love the way they look online. In person I was disappointed a little with some aspects. For reference I am 5‘,5“ tall and 115 pounds. My measurements are 26 inch waist and 38 inch hips. I ordered the size extra small and it fit perfectly. It was even slightly snug in the waist and hips on me, but not a dealbreaker. I was disappointed online the color is a very pretty light sky blue. However in person it’s a darker, dusty blue color. I already have pants this color and really wanted a light pale, blue pant. The other thing I didn’t care for are the front pleats they’re not very flattering and add a little bit of bulk to me. Finally they were also a little bit short. I’m only 5‘5“ tall and these fell right at my ankles to where I really need to wear flats with them in order for them to not look like high waters. I typically like my pants longer, and to cover most of my shoe. All in all they are an OK pair of pants. I wasn’t thrilled at the color in person is not the same and didn’t particularly like the front pleats but I will still wear them. Hope that helps!

Very thick, but fairly flattering and comfortable I was surprised by how thick these pants are, and I'm slightly concerned I will be too hot in them, but maybe when the weather cools, I'll appreciate that extra thickness. The outside layer isn't the coziest feel, but these pants are lined, and the liner is fairly comfortable and not scratchy. Because they are so thick, I didn't expect them to be flattering, but they're actually not too bad. They lie nicely and disguise anything unflattering with their interesting texture. Based on reviews, I went with the 29" and they are plenty long. For reference, I am 5'5". I think the longer length will only be necessary for those who are 5'7" or over, but that's just my opinion. Overall, a decent pair of drawstring pants.

Missing the flare I normally wear my jeans with a straight leg that are too small to take off with your shoes on. I liked the way these jeans looked in the ad. They look curvy with a flare at the bottom, and I thought I'd give them a try. These are a good-quality pair of jeans with five pockets. The front pockets are pretty deep, with a lining that feels soft and silky. The zipper is silver-colored, although it's a little stiff. Upon receiving them and seeing their shape, I could tell that they didn't have the flared or "bell bottom" style. I tried them on right away and then took them off even quicker. Being plus-size most of my life, I've always hated jeans like this. It's not just the bottom that's flared, but actually the whole leg. I've always felt that plus-size jeans were made with the thought that just because you have a larger waist, you must have legs the size of an elephant. A bell bottom I can handle, but not elephant legs. I used to wear the largest size I could find, but now I wear anywhere from a size 12 to 16 depending on the pants and the amount of stretch. I ordered these in size 16, and they are too big; plus, they are very stretchy on top of that. They're just too uncomfortably big without any kind of curvy shape to them. Additionally, they are too long for me, but I'm 5'5", and I usually run into this problem. Currently, 25% off is a fair price, and they're a good pair of jeans for someone; just not for me.

Being plus-size most of my life, I've always hated jeans like this. It's not just the bottom that's flared, but actually the whole leg. I've always felt that plus-size jeans were made with the thought that just because you have a larger waist, you must have legs the size of an elephant. A bell bottom I can handle, but not elephant legs. I used to wear the largest size I could find, but now I wear anywhere from a size 12 to 16 depending on the pants and the amount of stretch. I ordered these in size 16, and they are too big; plus, they are very stretchy on top of that. They're just too uncomfortably big without any kind of curvy shape to them. Additionally, they are too long for me, but I'm 5'5", and I usually run into this problem. Currently, 25% off is a fair price, and they're a good pair of jeans for someone; just not for me.

Love these! Tall-girl friendly I rarely give 5 stars but I love these pants. These are fantastic pants for warm weather! The material looks more expensive than it is and they feel great. I was nervous about the cream but they don't show through at all as long as you're not wearing dark undergarments. They are quite versatile and look great with a jacket for work or a tighter, shorter top for weekends. They feel like pjs on, and I work a lot from home/on the road so that's an added bonus. I wash them in cold and hang them to dry, and they don't require anything else. I also packed them for a trip (I travel a lot) and the wrinkles came out easily while I showered (I hung them in the bathroom and let the steam work on them). They are perfect for travel because they're so versatile and easy to care for. Also...pockets!!😃 I did order up based on the size chart, and I'm glad I did because I have some junk-in-the-trunk and I didn't want these to be tight and show off my flaws. I typically wear a US large (12-14)/Asian XL in pants and I ordered the XXL. They fit great. I could've worn the xl but I like the loose look of this size-- it is exactly what I wanted. For reference, I'm 5'10", 190 lbs, 38dd, 32 jeans, inverted triangle/apple shape. They're long enough for me, which means a petite lady may need to hem them.

Great Idea in Theory; Not Cute (On Me) in Reality 🤪 I really wanted to like these pants! I mean, they look SO cute on the skinny model!! 😋 But, on me -- they just look frumpy and make my body look bigger than it actually is. 😝 Unfortunately, these slacks just didn't hug me in the right places. For one, I'm almost always bloated and this waistband was WAY too tight, as in -- tight enough where I cut a slit on each side of the waistband so I didn't feel like a stuffed potato. 😂 For reference, I'm 5'2, weigh <125 lbs., wear a DDD bra size, and have defined, slender legs. These pants accentuated my belly, made my booty look flatter somehow (still not quite sure why....?), and didn't do anything to help my legs stand out...at least in a positive way! 😝 Although very comfy and flowy, I feel like these pants made my legs look bigger than they are. In addition, they were just a tad little too long while wearing flats, and they didn't seem to look right with the two different types of heels I tried them on with. While I will say the material is quite soft and comfortable, I wouldn't rely on these pants to keep me super warm when I'm feeling really cold. They'd be best suited to wear on days when there's a light breeze. Again, while I really wanted to like these pants, unfortunately, they're not a home run for me. But, if you're rather slender with a small tummy, you might love them. ✌️

The good, the bad and the rest Well... kinda on the fence... I went with the Size: X-Large | Color: Khaki. For reference I'm over 50 years old. Height 5'6", weight 170lbs measurements Bust: 43 Waist: 34 Hips: 43. These pants have a lot of orange/pink to them for being "khaki" depending on the lighting. But we all know khaki is one of those colors we all have different ideas of what it actually is. This fabric is very light weight, flowy, with some small stretch and they kinda look like a linen. They are 100% polyester though. That means they haven't shrunk or anything in the wash and they don't hold the wrinkles nearly as badly as linen does. But that also means they don't breath as well either. On the softness scale of 1-5, 5 being super soft, these fall around a 4. They are so light that they aren't often in contact with your skin unless you're sitting, but the texture has not bothered my sensitive skin at all. The stitching all seems fine, decent construction. The pockets are a nice feature. Maybe it's because of the size, but the front and back are not that different. And on me, it's unfortunate where some of the pleats fall from the elastic waist. It adds to the look of my belly. Not something I need exaggerated. With a shirt over it, it can look a little better but I will probably alter the waist line in time. Overall, they are pretty decent pants that would work in an office or for traveling. With the price being under $20, that bumps these an extra star. I would say they are worth checking out. Peace and comfy pants

The good, the bad and the rest Well... kinda on the fence... I went with the Size: X-Large | Color: Khaki. For reference I'm over 50 years old. Height 5'6", weight 170lbs measurements Bust: 43 Waist: 34 Hips: 43. These pants have a lot of orange/pink to them for being "khaki" depending on the lighting. But we all know khaki is one of those colors we all have different ideas of what it actually is. This fabric is very light weight, flowy, with some small stretch and they kinda look like a linen. They are 100% polyester though. That means they haven't shrunk or anything in the wash and they don't hold the wrinkles nearly as badly as linen does. But that also means they don't breath as well either. On the softness scale of 1-5, 5 being super soft, these fall around a 4. They are so light that they aren't often in contact with your skin unless you're sitting, but the texture has not bothered my sensitive skin at all. The stitching all seems fine, decent construction. The pockets are a nice feature. Maybe it's because of the size, but the front and back are not that different. And on me, it's unfortunate where some of the pleats fall from the elastic waist. It adds to the look of my belly. Not something I need exaggerated. With a shirt over it, it can look a little better but I will probably alter the waist line in time. Overall, they are pretty decent pants that would work in an office or for traveling. With the price being under $20, that bumps these an extra star. I would say they are worth checking out. Peace and comfy pants

Very nice pants for Fall/Winter These are such nice, corduroy pants! I got them for my granddaughter, age 15, who really liked the look of them on Amazon. She’s wearing an adult size 8-10, and is very picky about what she likes to wear for school. Meaning, they had to be black. Haha. Seriously, though, black is her signature color, as she proudly proclaims. She loves that these are cargo pants, and also that they’re corduroy, rather than plain old denim. The corduroy is thin-wale, and the fabric is very soft, making it a perfect, cozy feel for Fall/Winter. The pants fit her perfectly, too. The fabric has a nice bit of stretch in the waist, but not obviously so - it’s just enough to stretch over the hips, then fit her perfectly in the waist. The length is even great! So many pants are too long on even average-size women/girls. These are a tiny bit long on my 5’4” granddaughter, but she prefers them to be long and slouchy. She’s a tough sell these days, but she is honestly delighted with these pants. The price of $34.99, as of this writing, is very reasonable for a quality-made pair of pants. These are machine wash and dry, perfect for a busy family with 3 kids. I’m very pleased with these pants.

Overlook the wrinkles! I wish I had taken a picture of my purchase when I pulled it from its bag. Honestly, I was ready to return it. Instead, I washed it by itself, on the gentle cycle, shook it out really well and hung it to dry. The picture is the result and I love it. The fabric naturally has small wrinkles and I'm sure there will be more when I wear it (next week). I'll be going for "snappy casual". It is very comfortable and it has large pockets - not just tiny slits. I'm talking LARGE pockets. I'm 5'7" and the length is slightly above my ankles.

THESE WILL SHRINK AND BREAK YOUR HEART I ADORED these pants when they first arrived. I’m 5ft 4.5in tall, 185 lbs, and ordered the XL. They were the perfect, breezy, floor-length pants of my dreams. They skimmed my problem area (my postpartum belly) gracefully and made my stomach look flatter. I washed them once on the cold delicate cycle and hung them to air dry and they shrank at least 2 or 3 inches. They used to touch the top of my feet, and now they are above my ankles. I’m not sure if I’ll wear them at all now - instead of lengthening my proportions, they make my legs look shorter and stubbier. Extremely disappointing and a waste of money. Save yourself the heartache! Unless you’re exactly 5’2, in which case they’d probably end up being perfect lol. Ladies, know your fabrics. Anything 100% rayon or viscose will shrink. Look for polyester options instead. Check my other reviews if you’d like to see the brand I found that worked way better for me.

Stylish and Versatile with Unique Fabric These pants are super cute and versatile. The material is unique—almost like a ribbed, satin pajama fabric but thick enough to hide any lines or bumps, and thin enough to drape nicely. They can be dressed up or worn casually as "fancy sweatpants." I got them in black and I'm curious about the other colors, as the material might look different in lighter shades. If you're shorter, they might be too long for the ankle-tie style. I usually wear a large, and the large fits perfectly.

2X - 5’1 240lbs Not designed for short people. Fabric is comfortable and the stretch is high. They hold up well in washing machine and didn’t appear to shrink too much during a high heat drying cycle. Was hoping they would a little bit because they are too long for me.

Flowy, Lightweight, Beautiful Shade, Comfortable Elastic Waistband & Pockets I ordered these pants in size Medium, in the color Sail Blue, which is a beautiful shade that neither looks washed out nor so dark that you can't see enough of the soft blue tones...it's just right! The Lyocell fabric is soft to the touch and thin but not see-through, so these trousers will feel great on warm days. The fabric is very flowy in the wide legs. Wearing these pants is a comfortable experience. The nice wide, fully elastic waistband adds to the comfort, and the functional, deep pockets just make like easier all day long! For reference, I usually wear about a size 8 in jeans. My waist is about 29", my hips are about 38", I'm 5'4" tall and long-waisted (hence, I have a shorter inseam than some other people of my height). I may have been OK with a size Small, but I like how the elastic waistband of the size Medium feels. It's comfortable and not restrictive. The length is just a tad long on me, but it still works. The photos I've attached were taken after the pants were washed and dried on Medium heat one time. I've read that Lyocell could shrink about 3% during the first laundering and then not much afterwards. These are wonderful, smart-looking, comfy trousers that are a joy to wear!

My waist is about 29", my hips are about 38", I'm 5'4" tall and long-waisted (hence, I have a shorter inseam than some other people of my height). I may have been OK with a size Small, but I like how the elastic waistband of the size Medium feels. It's comfortable and not restrictive. The length is just a tad long on me, but it still works. The photos I've attached were taken after the pants were washed and dried on Medium heat one time. I've read that Lyocell could shrink about 3% during the first laundering and then not much afterwards. These are wonderful, smart-looking, comfy trousers that are a joy to wear!

Nice pants; a little too short for me I requested these pants on the chance that they are actually longer than the size chart indicates... but nope, the chart is spot on. I'm 6 ft tall, 139 lbs and I'm wearing a size Large; a Medium would have fit but I was going for baggy and long, which, together, are on-trend for FW24. They are really nice pants -- nice medium weight, full in all of the right places and the cargo pockets add a funky twist. These pants are a very nice length for someone of average height, but even positioned below my waist, they fall too short to be cute on me. They do have a half inch seam so I'll let them out. I think these pants are a great value -- really, really nice.

Soft & Comfy, Pricey, But Not A Great Fit For Pear-Shaped Women, I *WILL* Luncle Dry! I'm just under 5'8" and down to 195lbs at the time of my order. I lost over 60 lbs last year, from a size 22-24 to I'm not sure what size exactly. This might be important for others to know. I'm still losing slowly, but based on the measurements I took of myself before ordering (Waist 39", Hips 45", Inseam 32-34"), I optimistically ordered size 14 -- and miraculously, they fit, just a bit snug but not overly tight. I think the slight stretch to the material helped with that. The tag was so amusing that I had to share. I'm too old to deal with hand washing anything, so if it doesn't survive my machine, it's not worth having. You can bet that not only did I wash it in my machine (cold water, normal setting, with the rest of my clothes), I also tumbled it dry -- to be fair, I did follow the instructions to not luncle dry it! They came out just fine, with no noticeable shrinkage. If machine washing & drying makes them wear out faster, that's just too bad. The most I'll go out of my way for something special I rarely wear is to put it in a net bag to wash and hang to dry -- but that's only if I have the time and inclination. :) The only problem I have with them is the same as with many pants: there is a gap at my lower back since my hips & butt are bigger than my waist. I have to wear a belt with these pants to correct that. They are not really made for women with my type of curves. I'm also a notorious penny-pincher, and these pants are nearly $40, which is almost twice as much as I prefer to pay for pants. These are comfy & soft, but the material is also thin, so I don't know how long they'll last before they tear. I've only worn them a few times since I received & washed them. For this price, I hope they last a long time. If they do tear or have any problems prematurely, I will come back to update.

This will be the most HONEST REVIEW you've ever seen..... I bought the Black in size 12 and the Green in size 14, but only bcuz a Green size 12 didn't become available Until AFTER I let the seller know they were running out of sizes. However, I have tried Shrinking the Green size 14 and they did shrink a little bit. I will try to Shrink the Green some more, but I will need to Stretch the Legs while they are hot and/or Use a Steamer to Stretch the Length, bcuz I really don't want the Length to Shrink! Also, as you will see in the photos, the Black pair had Tags etc strung into a little black loop inside the back of the pants, whereas the Green pair didn't have any Tags nor a special little black loop for the Tags, so I almost kinda feel that the Green pair were a Return, but that's ok, bcuz I've since washed them... But truthfully, I'm shocked that these pants/jeans are as Amazing, Soft and Super Comfy as they are! When I first grabbed the package from being downstairs and outside at the door, it didn't feel like a package of jeans. It felt waaaaay too light for jeans and I was almost disappointed! But... I opened the package and decided to try them on anyway! Wow!! I am absolutely blown away! Not only do they Fit according to the Size Chart, (I'm 5' - 6.5" and about 145ish pounds) but they are a really nice, soft and a "comfy around the waist" pair of jeans, that I honestly think will become my Favorite pair of jeans!! Due to stomach issues, I don't usually like anything around or touching my upper tummy, but these are sooo Light and Soft that they honestly Don't bother my tummy 1 bit! I'm even been sitting and laying on the couch to check out their comfortness and they pass with flying colors.. COMFORT Beyond Belief!! And bcuz of how extremely Comfortable they are, I am now going to order a couple more pairs!I've already ordered and received the Green pair, but I'm going to get another Black pair like these ones, and I think I'll get a pair of the Darker Blue ones IF the seller has more of the Blue ones in a size 12?!? I do have 1 "Slight" issue, and it's nothing major, it's a "Me Thing" just bcuz I'm very picky when it comes to a Pants Length, I do prefer my pants to be Longer, and so, I wish these jeans were another Inch, or even preferably 2 inches Longer!! But as they are right now, they almost touch the floor at my 5' - 6.5" of height! And like I said, it's a "Me Thing", I just prefer Extra Long Pants!! However, after washing them and before hanging them up to dry, I'm going to try and Stretch the Legs out in order to Gain a little bit more Length!! And IF this doesn't work, I may have to use Hot Steam to Stretch them out a bit, bcuz Hot Steam always works when wanting to Stretch out clothing!! **I have since washed both pairs in cool water and tumbled dried them for a bit with a little bit of Heat and I was able to stretch the Length back.** Anyway, being an extremely Picky person, I personally and highly Recommend these jeans! You Won't be Disappointed!! I truly hope you Grow to Love them as much as I do, and I've only had them on for 30 minutes just to try them out... (I've now had them for 2 days and have washed both the Black and Green pair and All was good) But I do know already, that they will and have, become my FAVORITE pair of Cargo jeans... And yes, it's true that they are Not a Heavy Cotton jean, but that's why I Love them so much, bcuz with them being a lighter fabric, it makes them Easier to move around in! I can do Squats and whatever else in them, plus Sit for Hours in them and I'm still very comfortable and it's all bcuz of how Flexible they are with being a Lighter fabric/material... --Bottom Line, I have absolutely NO PROBLEM with these Cargo jeans! They are absolutely Amazing and I'm sure you will Love them as much as I do...--

Not Sure About These... I am 5'4", rather athletic build, and not much of a booty.😭 I hover between 145-150lbs. I bought a medium, which was what was suggested. I wear anything from a size 6 to a 10 in bottoms, although 8 is my overall average. These are VERY high- waist on me, but I have a short torso so that might be why. They aren't as loose fitting at I thought they would be, but they aren't tight. The waistband is very stretchy so don't panick when you first pull them out the bag. I thought they were child's pants at first! I bought the army green color and it is much darker than what is usually considered army green. It's almost like what would be called a "forest green". The pockets are fine although they land lower on my butt than I would like. Note that the little front patch is just that--a patch of material that is sewn on, not an actual pocket. The material is similar to a lightweight chino or khakis, good for fall and spring. With some leggings underneath you could wear them in the winter as well. Overall, they are very comfortable, I just am not sure how to style them. They aren't tight at all, so if you like how they look on me, get your regular size, but double check the size chart measurements.

Terribly unflattering on me I had high hopes for these pants but my goodness, they look awful on me. The material is nice--lightweight and cool, 100% cotton--and the style is cute. Winter weather trousers I ordered the The Drop Women's Standard Nancy Pleat Front Wide Leg Trouser to wear to work. I love the navy color with the white pinstripes. These are a nice trouser to wear to the office. The material is heavyweight and great for cold weather. The zipper and closures all work well without any defects. The front pockets are a good size, while the back pockets are just for the look. The legs are really wide and the length was good for my 5'7" height as they did not drag on the ground. The only issue I had with these pants is the waist seems to run a bit small. I normally wear an XL and these were slightly uncomfortable to wear to work. There is some elastic at the back, but I intend to move the clasp a bit to make them more comfortable. If you are between sizes, I recommend sizing up. They are pricey when compared to similar products as I am not familiar with this brand.

Flowy & comfortable palazzo pants, but long. The material has a really nice feel to it. Listed as 70% cotton/30% linen. They are flowy & full. Very comfortable. I am 5’4” & they are a bit long on me, but will be fine with a small heel. I mostly wear a size 12, occasionally a size 10 if an item runs large. I ordered an XL, but think a Large may of fit more appropriately, both in length & size. My suggestion would be to size down, if in between sizes like myself. They came in a vacuumed packed bag, so they were quite wrinkly, but steamed out nicely & easily. They are very pretty pants if a palazzo style appeals to you. The pockets are a decent size, but are not overly deep. I think they might look a little more swanky on someone taller than myself, but they are attractive pants. To me, the color is more of a periwinkle, than a true blue, but they are a pretty color. I wish there was a petite option, but these are stylish pants that can be dressed up or down. I would recommend, if fuller pants appeal to you.

Beautiful quality but will shrink! I think I have fallen in love with these linen pants. The quality is amazing. It’s lightweight when on and heavy when holding it because of great quality. This isn’t those cheap ones, these are the real deal. It’s very flattering and make your bum look fantastic. Pictures don’t do it justice. It also has real functional pockets as well as drawstrings. You could wear this low or high waisted and it looks good both ways. I must warn you though, it will shrink if you wash and dry them in the machine. They were long on me before washing but now they are shorter than I want. I think they are suppose to be almost below ankle length but I liked them longer especially since I’m barely 5ft tall and want to wear heels with them. Anyway I got the size XS in khaki (last picture attached is put against standard cardboard box color). If you want them more loose and long, go up a size especially if you wash them in washing machine and dry them in dryer. Overall, I still love them regardless if they shrunk. This is a must for spring, summer and early fall wardrobe.

Different Fit For Different Colors! I am 5'9" and a pretty skinny 126 lbs. I bought 2 pairs in the exact same size - Red, Brown, Medium. I returned the red. They did not fit the same neither were they both of consistent quality. The red fit smaller & were narrower/tapered in the legs. The material was poly/cotton & thinner. The brown was a great fit, long enough and thick, comfy material. They are 65% Rayon, 35% Spandex! That means they will never shrink in the dryer. I don't detect any stretching from the Spandex content, which is odd, but for me, not a bad thing. Haven't washed them yet, so we'll see how they hold up otherwise. Maybe these pants were made in different countries, but now I'm looking to get a different color that is exactly the same as the brown pair. If you have bought other colors & you see Rayon/Spandex mix, post it in your review. I'll def order that color. It seems that I can't post 2 different reviews, Amazon keeps giving me the first review to edit for the second pair. So I've combined both reviews here. 2 stars for the Red, 4 stars for the brown .... Guess I'll average to 4 stars so the browns don't reflect badly.

inconsistant Since I was reading about the discrepancy in the fit and concern of shrinking, I ordered two pair of capri-green, a medium and a large. As you can see, they're different color and different fabric, which just shows the inconsistency of this product. They feel different, as they're different fabric, but both are nice. The length of both are right above the ankle. I'm 5'4" 145 lbs. The medium fits me better, but the elastic waistband is a little tighter than I'd like it to be, and I'm worried about the shrinking as it's the 100% cotton one. The large on fits fine too though. Neither one says to dry clean. I'm keeping both.

They will shrink. I ordered three pair in prints and was not disappointed. The colors are vibrant and the patterns are beautiful.(5'7" / 160 /curvy). Ordered one size up, so XL would have been too small. The 2XL feels good. 4 stars not 5 because they did shrink and also Ithe fit on the waist, the elastic sits low on the hips if I want a full length. I tried them on before washing and they fit perfectly but I washed them and hung to dry and then 10 min on Low in dryer. They came out about 2" short on the legs and the overall fit more snug and is similar to sweat pants. The fabric is comfortable and not thin, but light on the body and translucent in the light. I'm happy and considering buying more.

100% cotton, dry clean recommended The size XL fit my 180 lb, 5'3" body. BUT girls with thick thighs like me won't get that baggy pant look you see on the models. That said the pants are nice. Elastic leg bands fall just above the ankle and the button detail is a nice touch. I didn't realize that detail faces forward, not to the side like I thought, but it's fine. Also from the pictures, I thought the pockets were wider/bigger which isn't a problem but just pointing it out. Fabric has a chemical smell so you need to wash before wearing. I'm holding my breath they don't fall apart in washer since professional dry cleaning is recommended for these 100% cotton pants, but the tag also says wash separately. I have to learn to read descriptions more carefully, but when I saw them I just had to have them, especially for only $19!! Ordered another pair in XXL to see if I get that baggy leg look.

L-O-V-E (and they have pockets!) Size reference: I am 5’6” and 126 lbs, wearing the size small recommended on the size chart. I normally wear a size 4 in dress pants and jeans and have a 27” waist and 37” rear. Fit is true-to size, and there is plenty of stretch in the waist if you are between sizes. I absolutely love these pants. I wore them the first chance I had to my business casual desk job. The material is gauzy, soft, and slightly stretchy. The waist is wide-band elastic with a draw string. The legs are really wide and the waist is high-high (like bottom of rib cage). They came without shipping wrinkles and did fine after all day wear. The legs are flowy and the high waist is flattering with a slim fit top. I wear tighter pants, skinny and slim mostly, for work most days so these wide leg palazzo pants are a nice change. I feel so comfortable while seated and stylish when I’m greeting people. Grace Karin delivered again and I’m certain I will be wearing this pair regularly. I can wear with accessories to work or could throw on a crop tank and sandals to wear casually on vacation. Either option is cute and comfy, 2 of my favorite things! Absolutely no complaints, 5/5 stars

So cute but SO long These pants are quite adorable. They are very Boho chic. I love the wide leg design with the tassels at the waist and the fact that they actually come with pockets that are decently deep. The material almost reminds me perhaps of high-quality bedsheets or maybe a really good handkerchief if that makes any sense. They are soft and very comfortable, but are very far from being short person friendly. I am 4 foot 11 and these drag the ground for at least 2 inches.. the model in the photo makes it seem that it comes right to their ankle so they would have to be quite tall for that to be true. I had hoped they would just come to the very top of my feet, or at least lay on the top of my feet, but they definitely drag on the ground past that. I will be forced to wear quite tall shoes with these, unless I can find a way to get them hemmed. Other than the issue with the link, these are quite incredible.

Exceeded expectation, love the fit Size reference: I am 5'6" and 129 lbs, normally a size 4 in pants/jeans. The size chart suggested a small but I wanted a loose waist so I went with a medium. I am pleased with the up-size! The legs are full length, the waist is not digging into me but doesn't look too big. Description: I have ordered many wide leg pants so I expected the fabric to be chiffon type and flowy. However, the material is firm polyester, like a blazer with some stiffness and shape. Not thin and not see through. I love the pleats on the waistline as it does a lot flatter the under-navel region. They stick out a bit which helps camouflage pooches and sharp hip bones. The hip pockets are big enough to be functional. The waistline is extra-high, which I like from the front. There is something weird with the back view, perhaps the faux pockets are in the wrong place proportionally. As for quality of construction, the pants are well made but not name brand dupe worthy. I initially ordered strictly as work pants. However, after trying them on I think they would be cute with a crop tank and new sneakers for the weekend as well. Recommendation: I love them. I like the way the feel, look, and fit. I've already added them to my watch list and will order the navy if they go on sale. The pale pink is perfect for the spring. At the current price of $29.99, these pants exceeded expectations.

Don’t feel like lightweight linen These are … okay, casual, elastic-waist black pants. They do not have the lightweight feel or look that I associate with linen. They feel more like a heavier cotton fabric I’d associate with chinos. The finishing is a little sloppy (see close up of back pocket). Size medium fits my tween daughter, 5’4” 115lbs, in the baggy way that she likes, and they are not cropped at her height — more like full length pants with some extra length allowing them to pool over the cuffs a bit.

Not exactly "linen-looking" or "linen-feeling"...especially for $35!!! Not a huge fan of these pants. 1. They are not what you would consider linen. When you think of linen you think of a thin, "flowy", wrinkly, lightweight, and breathable summer pant. These are none of these things...they are more like rather stiff chinos with texture. The description pictures definitely paints the picture of linen but it is deceiving and misleading in my opinion. 2. The pants went all the way down to the ground when I thought they would be above the ankles per the description picture (again, very misleading...this model must have been 5'11" or taller!!). I had to roll them 4 times to get them "cropped" looking and then they just looked weird. I am 5'6" and 190 pounds so I am not super short and I got size Large. I expected more for pants that are $35. I will wear them to work but they won't be my go to summer beach pant sadly :(

Easy to Wear Pants - Work or Casual These pants are very comfortable. I am just under 5'5", and about 125 currently. Bought a medium. They are a little big, but still okay. I wash them, but do not put in the dryer - just hang up immediately. They come out almost wrinkle-free this way. The material is a little flimsy / lightweight, so i don't anticipate wearing except in the summer. I added a few stitches with a sewing machine, because the elastic in the waistband kept rolling. Now the elastic can't "flip" inside the material. Sometimes i roll the waist-band down one time to create a shorter "rise", since these are truly a long rise.

I actually like these & they're incredibly comfortable - review from a fluffy mom I am reviewing this as a mom with a bit of a fluffy body (I am EXTREMELY self-conscious about my waist and belly so no photos of the waistband on), 5'5"ish and usually wear an 8/10 -- so definitely not young and cute like the model. I ordered a Large in these pants and I am very pleasantly surprised.First, they are a cotton-twilly material that doesn't feel particularly soft initially but actually moves pretty well when wearing the pants. They have a boxy feel -- but that is typical of the cargo look (and my lack of an actual waist). I give the waist band 100 stars -- it is a mix of elastic and button/zipper so it has some give, doesn't compress on my belly or slip below a muffin top and it and is really super comfortable. I won't show any photos, but the band comes right above my belly button and securely above my hips and covers the small of my back completely-- so they cover everything and sit in a very comfortable spot. Even when I sit they are very comfortable and stay in place above my belly and hips. There are belt loops too just in case you need them, but waist wise the size is perfect for me, esp with the elastic!Length wise, these seem to run a bit long for me -- however I actually really find the rolled-up style with the sandals cute. For tennis shoes I rolled the hem up once and I think they are okay. The boots photos have no roll. The sandals photos took 3 full wide rolls on the hem. These could easily be hemmed, but I often like my pants on the longer side so I can wear them with different types of shoes and in different season -- so I am good with these. But, yes, I would say they run long.Lots and lots of pockets -- 2 back pockets, 2 hip pockets, and 4 cargo pockets. The cargo pockets are real and not just decoration. The top ones just have a flap, the bottom ones have a button with the flap. So lots of storage if you want it.I actually surprised myself and like these. I am always nervous about ordering online because I never feel anything fits me in a flattering way, and I second guessed my order a million times. But now that I have them on and see how incredibly comfortable they are on even with my body style, I am really glad that I pushed order. These are a keeper and I would definitely recommend.

Nice fabric, nicely tailored. Good fit. I'm 5'3" tall, weight 150 pounds. This is a size medium. Plusses: They have the tailoring of the 1940s, with snug fit over hips and waist and flaring to the legs. The elastic on the waistband is wide and quite stretchable to get over hips to put on. Pockets are well made and have the placket to carry tons of stuff. The buttons are 5/8" across giving a nice decorative look. The pants are long for taller people, yet you can tie the legs to lift the hem above your shoes. Minuses: Wish the pockets were velcro closed as the buttons are hard to get through the buttonholes. And when unbuttoned, you can't see them at all. Strings on pant legs are long and could trip up the wearer unless double tied. Nice pair of cargo pants and will be my go-to for casual wear.

Not For Petites. Man, I was so excited for these ETOSELL Baggy Jeans, but sadly they are just not made for us Petites! I am 5'0 & 115 lbs (with a 28" waist & 40" hips) and usually wear a size 6, so that is what I ordered. These here jeans are just way too long for my short self and are also kind of too big everywhere else. I really loved the look of these jeans and even own another pair of ETOSELL Cargo Jeans in a size 6, which fit me really well, so yeah I was definitely disappointed that the fit of these were so off compared to my other pair from this brand. The jeans are made well, they are heavy, do not have stretch to them, but are comfortable. I would not recommend these jeans if you are on the shorter side like myself. I would possibly consider sizing down since these are so baggy? Maybe they would have fit a little better if I got a size 4, not totally sure though...just a thought!

The good, the bad and the rest Heavy weight denim used for these so should hold up. I chose the Dark Blue | Size: X-Large. For reference I'm over 50 years old. Height 5'6", weight 170lbs measurements Bust: 43 Waist: 34 Hips: 43. The fabric on these is better than I was expecting. No stretch at all, but baggy enough not to interfere with movements. I also like that the color is uniform. I don't care for the worn out faded in patches look. The inseam is about 29.5". The stitching and construction all seem solid. And the front pockets are nice and deep, can even hold my kindle 7. The crotch fit is a little weird on me, but that's due to my shape. You're experiences will probably be different. In general, these are good jeans. Peace and sturdy pants

Poor quality, didn't love the fit I am 5'2" and hippy/curvy and have a bit of a butt. These pants fit true to size when following the chart, but I found that the crotch area was very unflattering and only wear these with a top that will cover the front. It puckers a bit, which makes me wonder if they're not more flattering on someone with a slimmer/straighter build than mine. Also out of the package, my jeans had damage on the seam that goes down the front of the legs. You can see it in the photos I provided. For the price, I find that kind of quality issue to be unacceptable. All things considered the quality of these jeans felt like something that shouldn't be more than half the price listed, in my opinion. I wouldn't recommend these if you're looking for a well-made quality product.

Large = 28 to 34-inch waists. 40-inch hips OK. I ordered Large because size chart says it's for 28-inch waists. The waistband of Large stretches from 13-inches to 19-inches (14 to 20 once worn). Large will thus fit anyone with a 28-inch waist (relaxed) all the way up to a 40-inch waist (fully stretched). Technically, you want to be on the relaxed side, and I would say 28 to 34-inches is fine for Large. I have 40-inch hips and Large fit fine despite size chart saying it's for 33-inch hips. The material is very stretchy. I posted some pictures showing that white is very see-through, and if you stretch these enough, you will even see camel toe silhouette as the material is very light and thing. and I recommend wearing a white slip-short underneath. I'm 5'6 and the pants are too long, and I'd have to put on some really high heels so they don't touch the ground. A 31.5-inch inseam, for high-waisted pants, should fit fine on my low-waisted 29-inch inseam, but these are too long.

Perfect Sizing but Not Real Denim Order the size 2 regular. For reference my measurements are 24.5" waist and 35" hip. The jeans fit perfectly around my waist without needing a belt which is a rare occurrence for me. Besides the sizing, however, the jeans are way over priced and the color is off. As you can see, I ordered the "regular blue" yet, the actual color that they came as is not as blue as pictured and actually seems like what the "light blue" option looks like. The material is also not real denim, so there is no vintage look to them at all. The material is like a jegging: super stretchy. Also, the button is really flimsy and seems like it will fall off easily. I returned them because $46 dollars for these is over priced.

Trendy, cozy jeans I'm one of the last of the millennials to switch up from my beloved skinny jeans into something more "trendy" and I honestly spent hours and went from store to store to store in malls and shopping centers to find things, only to be disappointed by the fit, color or the sizing. Amazon was my last effort so I went on a hunt and found these. They are trendy, cozy, and I feel extremely fashionable and relaxed in them all at the same time. I am 5'5" and weigh 121, I'm a Levi's 6/28 and with more of a booty than anything else, so finding pants that fit me well is always hard. Not these! I got a size 06. They're perfect for anything! I can wear a belt or go without, I can do heels or sandals, dress them up or down for any occasion. I'm definitely a fan! The only downside is that they REEK of leather. I washed them with an extra rise on heavy cycle and it still smells heavily of leather. My legs even smell like leather after wearing them. I'm about to throw them into the washer for the second time since Saturday (today is Wednesday) in an attempt to at least dial down the aroma. We'll see how it goes. Regardless of the smell, they're a fabulous pair of pants and definitely worth it. If you're like me and have been looking for something to switch up your style to fit in a bit more without compromising comfort, give them a try!

Great fit, just a little short Ordered these in the coffee color, size 4 for my daughter - she's 5'5" & 112lbs. The material feels nice, soft with a little stretch, and the thickness is just right - not too thin & not too thick. The construction is well done with neat seams, sturdy zipper, and easy buttoning. As far as the fit goes, these were perfect before washing & drying. The length was just right. After washing & drying we both thought they shrank too much lengthwise, and will look weird if she wears these with any type of heeled shoe. So, if you're on the tall side, don't put these in the dryer! Also, the color is solid brown, not the acid washed look in the pics on the product page. Four stars because they're too short & color is misleading.

Great fit, just a little short Ordered these in the coffee color, size 4 for my daughter - she's 5'5" & 112lbs. Four stars because they're too short & color is misleading.

Pant legs are different lengths! I ordered these pants with the hopes of wearing them on an upcoming Europe trip. Initially they arrived very wrinkled and long so I steamed them a bit to see what they would truly look like on and this did help shorten them a bit as well. I loved how they truly look like they're fully linen. The waist and proportions of size Small comfortably fit my 5'3" frame just right. The biggest issue though was that one pant leg was longer than the other! In trying them on with shoes, it was visibility apparent that one side reached the ground but the other wasn't. I tried adjusting but the difference was still obvious. Laying the pants down, you can see in the photos how the uneven hemline is up to an inch different. Returned as defective and hopefully the replacement will not have this issue!

Cute but a bit uncomfortable to wear These pants are super cute, but they are a bit uncomfortable and itchy to wear. They have a layer of white fabric that is meant to be a barrier between the sequence and your skin, but the fabric is very thin and porous, and unfortunately the sequence just pokes through making the pants very itchy while you wear them. Other than that, they are well made, and very pretty. They are stretchy and they fit as expected. I am 5'1 so they are a little long on me, but that is expected. Overall, these are very nice pants but they are not the most comfortable to wear. They are itchy and thin, but they are stretchy and pretty.

Ok sequins!! Okay. So. Sequins are kind of my thing. I'm a singer and have been wearing sequined apparel for 10+ years. I'm familiar with all the common issues (improper inner lining so it's scratchy, sequins coming loose and getting snagged, falling off, bending, etc). In many cases, pants in this style are EXTREMELY scratchy due to thin lining and are highly unpleasant to wear. Not the case here - they are soft, stretchy, and comfortable enough to wear for hours (which is usually how long I'll need to wear them for so that's good!) Additionally, most of the time sequined pants highlight my legs in ways I'm not a fan of, as I'm on the curvier side. They usually make me look heavy and short. Due to the flare, these are very flattering and will look great with a cute top or cropped blazer. The only issue is that they're long. I'm 5"1' so most pants are obviously going to be too long on me. These are probably a good 3-5 inches too long and unfortunately it won't be remedied with high heels. However, I'm so in love with the way these look that I'm planning to get them hemmed so I can wear for gigs - it's worth it!

Love the pants! A little bit "tall" FYI Reviewed by spouse: These are so fun, I cannot wait to wear them! I think you could pull these off with a concert T-shirt and sneakers, and wear them during the day or dress them up to wear to a concert or out and about and they would be just a great asset to your wardrobe. I would not call these brown, as the sequins is gold with black netting between them that makes the pattern. These are quite versatile, I think, and could act as a neutral with other things on top. I’m wearing the medium and I think this is the correct size for me. For sizing reference, I’m 5’3 and 155. They are quite stretchy, so I think they could accommodate someone 165-170 without having to go up to a size large. They are quite long on my frame and I will need to wear either a 3-4 inch heel or get them altered if I want to wear them with sneakers.

Just not quite what I envisioned I'm 5'5, curvy fit, 135ish. Not long legged. I normally wear a 27/6 in jeans ... I sized up to an 8 in this. Nothing was tight but I didn't live the look. I've seen barrell jeans (not tried on) and mom jeans--each a baggy look. I do own the Free People knit jumper and a FP denim oversized set of bibs and like both. Each of those are an obvious oversize. These jeans, on the other hand, just were not baggy looking as the model shows. I read many reviews and hoped they'd work but they literally look like my mom wore in the mid 80s and hers fot about like this. Just not a cute look, imo, if they are not baggy enough. Go big, or go home. I still wear skinny jeans. I have wode keg jeans. I have cropped flares. I cannot wear straight leg or boyfriend style bc I'm a curvy fot (the waist always sticks out too badly). Providing this info, as I think it is all relevant. Last, I'm almost 50. This look might seem cuter on a gal my size but in her 20s. Or with no curve and less boots. Material was fine; blue color a tad off, seemed almost frosted-ish.

Size up if you’re curvy I haven't worn jeans in ten years. I had these in my cart for months and rly happy I decided to finally get them! They fit true to size. For reference, I’m 5’6”, 175lbs. 32 waist, 45 hips. Usually I’m a size 12/14, but I ordered these in 16 for a looser fit and glad I did! The front pleats are well placed for a flattering fit, especially if you’re curvier. The material is fairly thick, but also stretchy. Surprisingly comfortable! They hold up well in the wash & don’t shrink when dried. The rise hits at just the right spot — although the waist is a little baggy on me, so I have to wear a belt. Very pleased with the quality. I never spend more than $20 on pants, but these are worth the money for sure. 10/10 recommend. If you’re questioning getting them, do itttt:)

Buttery soft inside and out. These are SOOOOO me! I got the ones with the skulls on them. These are a lightweight material. Definitely not for wearing in the snow. Will be perfect for summer and workouts as your skin will be able to breathe. Although I said it's a thinner material, it feels substantial and well made. I shared a few photos showing with a flash on that you can't see my underwear. Stitching feels like it won't come apart. And will last through activities not just relaxing or running errands. It feels really buttery on the inside just like the outside. Very comfortable to wear. I'm 5'2" and the legs go down to my feet, but I'm not walking on them like sometimes I can have happen because I'm short. I can walk around barefoot and not have to depend on shoes to keep them off the ground. Being I have a short torso, the waist goes about an inch above my belly button. So if you have a longer torso, this should be great for you and you won't have to be concerned about bending over and your underwear showing in the back. I will say, while the leggings themselves have a decent amount of stretch to them, do pay attention to the waist measurements. Because the elastic band doesn't. So if you have wider hips or backside, you could have a issue getting over them. So measure those too and make your size choice. Totally recommend.

It feels really buttery on the inside just like the outside. Very comfortable to wear. I'm 5'2" and the legs go down to my feet, but I'm not walking on them like sometimes I can have happen because I'm short. I can walk around barefoot and not have to depend on shoes to keep them off the ground. Being I have a short torso, the waist goes about an inch above my belly button. So if you have a longer torso, this should be great for you and you won't have to be concerned about bending over and your underwear showing in the back. I will say, while the leggings themselves have a decent amount of stretch to them, do pay attention to the waist measurements. Because the elastic band doesn't. So if you have wider hips or backside, you could have a issue getting over them. So measure those too and make your size choice. Totally recommend.

Comfy but not polished looking at all For me, these would be fine as pajama pants that I would not wear out of the house. But at this price, I wanted a more polished look, maybe something comfy to wear while traveling. These are not that. The particular pair I received also had a strange bump sticking out where the waist band makes a V - no amount of adjusting made it go away. As mentioned by others, the fabric has tiny ribbing that isn't super obvious in the pics. I'm 5'9" and if I wear them below my waist line they are just long enough. If I wear them high waisted as marketed, they are definitely too short. Fabric is thin, clingy, and shows it all, so if you've got any lumpy bumps like me that you prefer not to advertise, these are not for you. However, if you're looking for lightweight, comfy house pants and have the need to spend almost 35 dollars on them, you're in the right place.

So cute and comfy, but shrunk in the wash I got this in both Apricot and Khaki XS and am in love. They’re so so comfy and cute and I feel like they can actually be dressed up or down depending on what you’re going for. It feels like you’re wearing pajamas but they still look elevated and presentable. They have a linen look with very little stretch (they’re so loose that I wasn’t bothered by lack of stretch). The tag says they’re 70% cotton and 30% linen. For reference, I’m 4’10”, 108-110 lbs, 26-27” waist, 36-37” hips and I had to get around 4” hemmed for these to fit as they do in the photos. WARNING: the attached photos were all taken prior to washing; after washing according to the tag’s care instructions (cold machine wash and line dry) they appear to have shrunk around 1 inch, which is unfortunate considering I paid to have these hemmed and apparently could’ve gotten an inch less and avoided this.

It’s now October and I have been wearing these pants (plus I bought a beige pair) all summer. They were perfect! Comfortable and cool but also provided a chic and casual look. They have maintained their quality and aren’t showing any wear. I just bought a pair in black for Fall. Definitely recommend. I have been looking for some cute and casual pants I can wear during this coming summer. I ordered the teal in small and I’m happy with them out of the packaging. I think these will work well for what I want from them. They fit great (nice and loose without looking too baggy). The length is just right for wearing sandals or flats. The fabric is light and breathable without feeling too cheap or flimsy. The teal color is more of a denim blue than teal. I was a little disappointed with the color difference (wanted a more green teal like the photo) but when I put them on my boyfriend immediately complemented me on the color (he loves blue). I haven’t washed them yet. I will update if they shrink or fall apart after a few washes.

To Size Up Or True To Fit? Going through the reviews it was challenging to decide whether or not to size up or not. If you have thicker legs and a booty, I would size up so you have a bit more room and it gives you the wide leg look. If you have thinner legs, I suggest purchasing your normal size. I’m 5’5” and 155 pounds, pear shaped. Pic 1 is a large and pic 2 is an extra large. I kept the xl and returned the l, normally I wear an 8. There’s not much of a difference outside of it giving me more room in the hips and bum area. The added benefit of sizing up was to minimize the panty line showing or any marking. The label says cotton and linen, it’s a medium thickness fabric. This is the perfect, lounge, work or travel pants. I loved it enough to buy a second pair in apricot. Oh! If you’re shorter than 5’7” you’ll probably have to get the pants hemmed. Sharing for mental preparation.

Comfy Casual but Put together 5’3” 29” waist ~145lbs Fabric : Nice drapey linen blend(?) fabric does well in heat and humidity, and has a flattering flow to it. Fit: has a real draw string, with no hidden elastic waist, so they are easily adjustable. I ordered the XL, for a drapier fit, and they did shrink a bit in length after the first cool wash. Definitely wash on cold to avoid shrinkage. Color & Transparency: Color is as pictured, slightly transparent but wearably so- i have tattoos on my legs and bum and if pulled tight you can see them, but for normal wear they’re just fine. You’re not seeing right through them. Overall very versatile, good for hot and humid weather, comfortable and flowy, and look really put together with monochromatic looks. Can be dressed up or down a bit depending on what you pair with them. I’d say they’re a good value.

The Pants For ANY Occasion I have these pants in rust and apricot and I really love them. For reference: I am 5' 6" and about 125lbs. Pros: drawstring waist, lightweight, pockets, flattering for a flatter figure despite being a more flowy cut. They can be dressed up or down - I can wear them with almost any crop top...so great for travelling if you want to pack light but still have options. I could wear flats without tripping on the hem. Pants of the spring / summer! I got these for a trip to Jamaica this Spring and I absolutely loved them. I've worn them many times since - dressed up to work, casual drinks with friends, and to travel in. They are very comfortable and light to wear in hot temperatures but they also have some weight to them to be comfortable when it isn't hot. I am 5'4 130lbs so they are a tad long when I wear shoes without a heel but I also have offset that by wearing them higher on my waist. Highly recommend these!

Good looking pant but mine has a flaw These are great looking pants. I like the style, the color and the linen look. Because the fabric is 60% rayon and 30% linen, the pant should be breathable and will be great for hot weather wear. I am 5’ 5” and 158 lbs. I usually wear an XL but because I wanted to make sure of a loose flowy look, I ordered a 2XL (a 1XL was not available). This 2XL is comfortable, and fits at my waist but they are about 3” too long in length. They are very loose and flowy on me but because of the design they do look nice.There were loose threads both inside and outside at the seams which I snipped off, otherwise the stitching is straight and unbroken. The wide band is comfortable and well sewn. The pockets too are well sewn and lay nicely.Here is the reason that I am rating it three stars and this may just be a flaw in my pant but I caution others to check the fabric closely. On my pair at the upper back hip on one side is a material flaw. In the photo, I placed a penny for perspective. It is very small and probably wouldn't be noticeable. My concern is that because it is a thin thread spot in the fabric and being at the hip location, will it form a small hole from sitting. I am really disappointed by this. I would have given a much higher rating had there been no flaw.

Doesn't Match Size Chart This review applies to all three pairs of FTCayanz "100% Linen" Pants. I used the size chart and ordered 2X. I tried them on, but instead of being a little loose, they were tight around hips and thighs. I will have to return, and I suspect that incorrect measurements is the reason for "frequently returned item" warning by Amazon. Before receiving, I thought maybe it was because of the fabric. The tag says they're 100% linen, but they feel a little too "silky" to be 100% linen; I'd guess they might be a linen-rayon blend. I own other linen & linen-cotton blend & linen-rayon blend clothes & I also do yarn work, so I am familiar with fabric texture. Even so, they're quite soft & I had hoped they'd fit. They did not. Much too tight. Hips were supposed to be 52" and I have 50" hips, so there should have been plenty of room to spare, they shouldn't have been tight at all. The legs are "straight leg" from hip to hem, so not for those of us who have meaty thighs. Unsightly in being revealing of lumps and bumps-- one reason to wear linen is to help hide that! The pants are on the thinner side, which means some transparency. The other thing to know about these pants is they do not have real back pockets, it's fake, but does look nice. I can't speak to value for money or shrinkage, but I wager even hand washing & line drying there'd be shrinkage (especially if there is rayon in them). Use caution. I'm 5'2" 220+ pounds, evenly distributed & consistently wear 2X during my slow weight loss. These 2X fit more like XL. Unfortunately, you can't trust their measurement chart, so buy at your own risk if you're plus size. I'll be returning all 3 pairs. The striped pair almost fit, and the dark grey was just slightly looser than the linen color pair-- inconsistent measurements even though they're all labeled 2X.

Good quality and comfy! I have these in black coffee (thick), beige (thin), and black (thick), all ordered in XS short. I’m 4’10” and had to get the black coffee hemmed 2” and the other two hemmed 1.5” to get them to the height seen in attached photos. My waist is 26-27” and hips are 36-37”. These fit me really well. The back has stretch to help with fitting. For reference, I’m wearing slingback pumps with a 2.5” heel and new balance 530s (~2” heel). The pants go well with a variety of looks. You can dress up and down as you want. Really good value for money. When I took the pants to get tailored the tailor complimented the quality and asked where they’re from! As for the difference between thin and thick - yes, the thin version is thinner, unsurprisingly. The thinner pants feel more like spring/summer pants and are a bit more breathable, while the thick pants have more weight to them. They both feel pretty soft to the touch. The thin pants have a metal clasp and the thick pants have a button closure.
