You can dress up and down as you want. Really good value for money. When I took the pants to get tailored the tailor complimented the quality and asked where they’re from! As for the difference between thin and thick - yes, the thin version is thinner, unsurprisingly. The thinner pants feel more like spring/summer pants and are a bit more breathable, while the thick pants have more weight to them. They both feel pretty soft to the touch. The thin pants have a metal clasp and the thick pants have a button closure.

Wow, super impressed I wear professional dress for work and have lots of dress clothes. Took a chance on these and I’m so glad I did. They feel and look very high quality. The XL long in dark green is what I got. The color is rich, the fabric is super thick but still drapes nicely because it’s kind of a smooth/slippery/dressy weave. I’ve never had this particular fabric but I like it for dress pants. I’m 5’7. Not sure if the wide leg and pleats would work as well on shorter people, but in size long they drape nicely, especially with a bit of a heel. The color is rich. The length is long without being ridiculous. The pleats camouflage the hips and the elastic in the waist means no gap, which is usually a problem for me. If you are wide in the waist it might be too tight. Love the options for thick or thin material. Overall I’m really impressed and plan to get a couple more pair in other colors. *I think they actually look better in person than in the photo I posted.

Differences between fabric styles is important! I bought two pairs to try on. My body: 5’2”, 130lbs, 29” inseam, Typically Size 2-4/XS-S. Pair 1: Army Green, Size S, Regular Length, Thick Fabric. Pros: Standard “dress pant” thickness, great length for me to wear with either flats or small heels/wedges. Beautiful green color, nice earth tone. Cons: Tighter in the center, felt like they hugged my body between the inseam and belly button tighter than the Khaki pair. Only two front belt loops, no back belt loops. Button front closure, not a “hidden button” like the Khaki pair. Would prefer to wear a belt with this pair to disguise that. Pair 2: Khaki, Size S, Shorter Length, Thin Fabric. Pros: Loved the thin, moveable fabric. Pockets and elastic waist sat flatter than than on the green pair. Hidden button closure looks better than the exposed button. Stitching looks better quality. Cons: Too short for me to comfortably wear with the wedges. Khaki color is not as flattering on me as the green. Overall: If the Army Green color was in stock in the thin fabric in my size, I’d order that instead. I much prefer the hidden button closure of the thin fabric and the structure seems to be better quality. They also look like a higher end product in person. However, due to the length and color not being right for me, I am only keeping the Green color and wearing those for my event instead of the khaki. I would recommend these pants overall and they are a great item to have in the closet! The biggest downside is that there are only two front belt loops. For the thin fabric with the hidden button closure it is less of a problem, but the exposed button really could use a belt to pull the outfit together. Overall I didn’t notice a size difference between the two, but the thin fabric felt like it was more flattering. I’ll probably wear shaping shorts under these just for my own comfort. Definitely need to wash (instructions say cold, gentle cycle or soak for a short time, hang dry) and steam out the wrinkles from the packaging before wearing!

So chic and classy, perfection!!! PETITE FRIENDLY I am 5' approx 125lbs. These fit so perfect! I got 5 colors, ALL IN SMALL REGULAR. I got the: Off white thick Black thick Khaki thin Pink thin Black coffee thick I'm glad I went with my gut/some reviews & didn't get 'short'. They all fit the same for the most part. Perfect around my waist with comfortable stretch left, but it wasn't loose it was fitted stretch left if that makes sense? Length was good on all of them except for the black coffee, that one needs to be hemmed and is super long. The rest fit perfect WITH heels on, which was my intent. If I want to wear them with any other shoe I will need to get them hemmed 100%. As far as thick vs thin goes, they feel like regular pants, neither thick or thin, I would have to wear the opposite to tell the difference. Although, I did wear the 'pink thin' out and you could see my cellulite LOL so maybe because it's thin. The black coffee is thick, the only one that does feel a bit warmer to me. The rest I would consider regular pants. The red heels in the photos are kitten heels, so not that high and the pink pants were above the ground. I tried to be very detailed! I hope this helps you! I definitely recommend so classy and chic!!

Super cute Cute versatile piece! I got them in two colors. One in the thin fabric and one in the thicker fabric. They fit and feel very similar but I do like the slightly thicker fabric just a little more. I’m 5ft, 168 lbs. I got an L short which fits perfectly.

Quality, Comfort, Value —💯 These pants are fantastic! I was so surprised at how much I love them. They are soft and oh-so comfy! I got the lightweight version and they are exactly that—lightweight, yet not see through. Absolutely perfect for summer! They look so much more expensive than what they cost. Thrilled to add these to my wardrobe. Don’t know about durability yet as this is my first time wearing them, but they seem like they’ll be ok in the wash. My only CON—and not a dealbreaker—is that there’s this funky closure. There’s a normal tab closure with a hook & eye, but then there’s a faux belt with the double rings where you pass the fabric through and tighten it by pulling the end—I hope that makes sense. Anyway, it was only on the front by the tab closure and cheapened the pants significantly so I removed it (of course before I thought to take a pic). Plus, idk how I’d wear a belt on top of it. Idk why the manufacturer would put that there. It’s not shown in any picture either. Again, not a dealbreaker, but wanted to mention it so it’s no surprise like it was for me. Here are my stats...I’m 5’4” and I’m 135 lbs. My weight is pretty evenly distributed, so I’m a natural hourglass. I’ve got a slight mom tummy going, but it’s not awful. My measurements are 39” (or 34DD), 29”, 39”. I ordered the SMALL in lightweight white. I hope you found my review !

cute slacks if you dont wanna splurge I'm 5'1.5 with a long torso so my biggest problem is the "high waisted" needs to go beyond my belly button. these pants are good if: - you're looking to not break the bank - need thigh/leg room in your pants - you have a long torso like me - need stretch to accommodate a growing body - you're over ~5'2 i returned them b/c: - material is not giving in my opinion, not terrible either just wasn't soft, comfy - the pants are kinda heavy - i wore a 1in heel with the pants on and it's still kinda long - didn't give me any body shape - not even close to the A&F Sloane pants / Aritzia dupe TLDR: not for the 5'1 girlies unless you want it a little long, decent material just not my preference.

I'm going to buy this in more colors! For reference, I am 4'10" and I bought an XS. These pants are a bit long when I stand barefoot, but are perfect when I stand on my tiptoes or when I wear heels. I think I will hem the bottoms just a bit, but otherwise I absolutely love these pants and I will be purchasing them in multiple colors. The scrunched waist band in the back grabs my waist and keeps the pants tight to my body. There is a lot of room in the thighs and I feel like I can move around! The material is thick enough that I do not have to worry about underwear showing through, but I also bought these in July in Southern California and am able to walk around in them comfortably without being too hot! I am so excited to wear these to work as well as to semi casual things.

Great price and quality This is my second pair of these trousers. Both in the thick fabric. This green pair I ordered a size L, the other pair I ordered in an XL. I’m around 180 pounds, 5’1. I have a very high waist and short torso and long legs for my height (note: my legs are still short AF, but since my torso is so short and my waist so high my legs at least look longer than my 5’1 height would make you think). The XL fit nice and were comfy and flowy, but they sat lower than my natural waist which annoyed me. The L fits exactly at my waist. They are a touch snug though, not uncomfortably so, but if there was a size inbetween the L and XL they would be perfect. I got the regular length in both sizes. The length in the L is perfect, they are a touch long on the XL since they sit below my natural waist but both work well with boots/heels. The XL works with flats/loafers if they have a platform sole. The only negative is that they don’t flatter my big bubble butt the way I’m used to with different style pants but the booty doesn’t ALWAYS have to be pippin’ I suppose. Regardless, love these pants. I haven’t tried the thin fabric style yet.

great for short girls! These are SO flattering for short girls. Bought the Medium Short in both Thin and Thick materials (I am almost 4’11”). TRUE short pants are hard to come by that don’t look bad - these are so flattering. The waist is tighter, which I like. If you are looking for a more loose fit in waist, size up. I like the Thin-Black pair most. I think that the Thick- will wrinkle more in the lower stomach area. Both are extremely comfortable. PSA: The THIN and THICK materials have different button-fastenings. Thick has a true button on the front. Thin has a fasten-hook on the inside. The true-button on the thick material may need you to sew on the button better.

Pants are amazing when they ship you the right product. Customer Service is garbage I bought these pants in another color, 31" inseam, and LOVE them. So I bought a second pair in Heather Grey and they arrived 2 inches shorter than they were supposed to be. Contacted Amazon who sent a replacement, another 29" pair came marked as 31". So I reached out to G4Free customer service who apologized and said a 2 inch discrepancy shouldn't have happened and gave instructions on how to measure an inseam just in case. I've written multiple polite responses saying that I followed their directions and the pants are 29" but marked 31" and they have ghosted me. So, if you get the size you order, they're amazing. But their product inventory apparently is inconsistent and customer service will leave you hanging. I won't be ordering more unfortunately.

Back for another pair! I originally bought the deep gray in a Large. I'm 5'5, 185 pear shaped with a long torso. These were SO comfortable and looked great with a pair of heels. I ordered them because none of my actual dress clothes fit and I dont' like feeling "stuffed" into clothes. As you can see from the photo, no one could tell they were yoga pants. The faux zipper and added front pockets help with that. I love them so much, I'm back for an additional color. They have more of a polyester feel as opposed to the pure cotton feel of most yoga pants. I like this feature as they do not pick up dog hair as easily (and yes, I have a dog). They travel well and do not wrinkle. I did use a little starch to put a crease them which helped with them looking like actual dress slacks. Great quality for the cost.

I'm 5'2", fluctuate between XS and Small. These pants after one wash hit right at the bottom of my heel without shoes, so no drag. Not too tight at the waist which I really like. Comfortable to wear even with any bloat. The fabric is not too thick or thin. Pant legs are wide enough that leggings could be layered underneath during the winter. The fabric is an athletic material, and stretchy. Pockets are decent size. You can see the shape of anything placed in the pockets, so not too much structure. Stitching is fine but not great. I expect these to fall apart within a year or two. As you can see in the 3rd pic, there's already some pilling and I've only washed these twice so far. Overall, these are petite friendly and ok value for the money. This is the first time I've bought from this brand. I'm not converted into a brand advocate and am not overly impressed. Won't go out of my way to look at their other offerings. But if I want another pair of these or come across something from this brand, would also be fine with buying. Content with this purchase.

Petite friendly! Comfy and flattering! Are you really short and hate having to hem pants? Are you slim thicc or carry your weight on your lower half? These are for you!! I’m 5’0” and fluctuate between 110-115. I ordered these in 29” XS and they fit like a GLOVE. No weird baggy crotch and THE perfect length to wear with my gym shoes without dragging. Fits my waist AND my large rear end… I think my butt looks cute in these but since I picked a light color, I can see dimples… I imagine this would not be an issue with darker shades. To be totally fair, I have not yet worn these extensively so I will update later if need be but I had to hop on here and let the really short ladies know to order these!!! I DREAD having to take stuff to the tailor and always pray I can find a pair that fits in the waist and in length… Hope this helps! **Updated original review to add pic of the back of the pants. Still loving these pants! I wash with detergent (no fabric softener) and hang them to dry!

Oh my goodness… First of all, the important part is I am 5’10” & 120lbs (in those photos), I bought a medium, tall, the 33” inseam, in white, khaki and black. And I am only wearing flats. Posting several pics to see the fit, plus for proof its not possible to take a bad pic in Europe. Anyway, I bought these to wear on our three week trip. I wanted something I could beat up but still look dressy & fabulous. Since we had never been to Europe and only brought a backpack each, I was really nervous about my clothes. But let me tell you, I would buy these pants all over again. I honestly felt like I one upped all the younger, high fashion girls in Milan and Capri. Also, my husband had me vacuum seal my clothes in my backpack. So they were (neatly) crammed into a super tight bag for three weeks. They went through six flights, unknown bus and trains and multiple ABNBs. Every time I pulled them out, they were wrinkle free. They are so soft, I actually slept in them, several times. They STILL do not have stretched out knees. They cleaned up super easy and air dried every where we went (cuz they don’t have dryers in Europe). I wore the WHITE in the RAIN on the streets of ROME (i feel really spoiled saying that) and figured that would be the ultimate test. I honestly figured I would be throwing them away when we got back. After 19,000 steps in the rain, the bottoms where they hit my tennis shoe heel had some discoloration after I washed them. But there has been no shrinkage and I still wear them. I wanted a loose, classy, palazzo look and the medium, tall did exactly that. I bought the small, tall and it was too tight on my bottom for what I wanted, so I gave them to my daughter who is about five pounds smaller. But the smalls did fit. The medium doesn’t feel like they are going to fall off, I felt confident in them everywhere we went. And the hips don’t look like they aren’t cut for a girl my size. I’ve purchased much more expensive pants and didn’t get 1/2 of what I am getting out of these. My only wish is they made them with a sewn in front seam and a non-yoga unwrapped waste for a more professional look. If they did, I’d probably buy the company.

Super comfy and warm! These sweats are exactly as described. The material is a slick feel on the outside that I am not familiar with but they are definitely fleeced lined on he inside giving that warm feeling. I wore them with an oversized sweatshirt and sneakers and even though I was in a baggy look; I felt super cute and comfortable. My inseam is a 33. For reference. I am in the process of losing prednisone weight but still deal with an arthritic inflammatory disease. I’m 5’8 and 188#;. Longer legs with a shorter torso, a perfect fit is large or size US 12. Weight is mostly in tummy area now; 13# left to go. I still go one size up for extra looseness in clothing but it’s almost too big now to do so. Shoe size is about a 7 wide when inflamed. 7 medium when normal. US shoe size 7-8 is about normal depending on shoe. FYI: online purchases I try to keep rating simple. If I was in a local store and could hold and view it; 5 star would buy and take it home, one star would put it back on shelf and pick something else.#OnlyJesusSaves.

Wear These Anywhere It's nice to have something to wear out that isn't work clothes. These G4Free Wide Leg Pants are just the ticket for me. Just under 5'5" and thick waisted, I was concerned whether or not I could find the right fit. I got the Lg and the measurements are spot on. The fit feels great and the length works perfectly. The stitching is pretty good, but these will probably be hand washed or in a wash bag to keep them nice. I don't really like the zippers. They zip up/down just fine, but they don't hinge well. In other words, you need to push them down so they don't stick up. Kind of weird, but they work. The pockets are roomy and lay nicely. You can't really see them unless you have your hand in one. Quite comfy. They have a sewn pleat down the front that doesn't stand out, just makes the pants look nicer. That also gives you a slimmer look, imo. Nice smooth feel. The waist size was smaller than I wanted, but actually fits great and they feel like they were made for me. I like them alot.

Sleek and comfy and great choice of inseam lengths These pants are fabulous for either lounging at home or at work.. The material is 81% polyester and 19% spandex, so there’s plenty of stretch and this material needs no special laundering, comes out of the dryer wrinkle free. What makes these pants look a little dressier to me is that sharp front seam. Very classy looking! The waist clings much like a pair of leggings. I think this is actually designed to be that way to lend shape to your silhouette. I don’t find the waist overly tight or uncomfortable. I like the zippered side pockets for an athleisure flare.The big attraction for me was the choice of a 29 inch inseam because I am petite and have spent my life shortening pants! I ordered a size small with the 29 inch seam and I am 5 feet tall, 117 pounds. These fit perfectly and just drape over the top of my shoe. I will enjoy this alternative style compared to jeans, joggers, or leggings on occasion. Great quality and I highly recommend!

Knee bagging and wrinkles I used to have a high opinion of the Gap brand, but my perspective has changed. I’m 5'4" and 150 lbs, and I ordered a medium, which fit me well. I liked it when I first wore it, even though the material felt thin. However, after just 4-5 wears, the pants started developing knee bagging. It’s also constantly wrinkled when I want to wear it, which is frustrating. The back pockets were sewn shut, but I managed to open them myself. I originally bought these for comfortable wear, but with the baggy knees and constant wrinkles, I lose confidence every time I put them on. Don’t buy these, there are better options out there

Do not size up… Size reference: I am 5’6” and 126 lbs. Almost all of my jeans are US size 4 or 27 and the majority of my elastic waist pants are medium (leggings, jeggings, joggers, sweats, etc.). The size chart suggested a small for sizes 4 and 6 but I decided to order my stretchy pant size of medium. Because of the cotton and rayon fabric, I decided to prepare for a little shrinkage. That was a mistake. Material: Thin, buttery soft, with just a hint of stretch. Lovely shade of army green with an elastic waist, functioning front hip pockets, and drawstring. Opinion: Definitely go by the size chart and downsize if you are on the line. These pants are so large on me that I can’t even cinch them with the drawstring to stay on. Way too big in the waist and baggy in the rear and legs. Honestly, the small probably would be extra roomy as well because these are not even close to my other mediums. They seem well made and I love the softness of the material. Definitely not a good choice for snow days but great for warmer weather. If they had fit, I would have used them for business casual attire at the office during the summer.

Too loose fitting in the waist and hips I really wanted to love these pants because I like their style and fabric but unfortunately they look unflattering on me. I’m 5’7” 150 lbs. I have relatively slim hips and thighs. My waistline is my trouble area where I carry most of my extra weight. I usually wear pull-on style pants in size large. These pants in size large are little too big on me. The waistband is too loose even with the adjustable drawstrings. When I walk it keeps loosening and I have to tighten the strings frequently. There’s too much space in the hips. I know these are not supposed to be tight fitting but on the model they fit in a relaxed yet flattering way and me they look frumpy and they make me look thicker as well as shorter than I am. I forgot to take a picture from the back but that’s where they look the worse on me. Also, when I’m sitting there’s too much fabric in the hips level that bunches up unflatteringly in the front. These are not for me but I’m still giving them 4 stars because I think that if you can find a size that fits you correctly, they could actually be nice, casual, breathable, lightweight pants.

Go by hip measurement I ordered a Small PETITE in indigo. I am 5 feet tall and wear a size 4. I was hoping they would fit shorter, however they do hit at ankle. Pants fit loosely, I would say closer to a 6 in the waist (chart says 4-6) but because of drawstring in waistline they can be pulled in. My hip measurement from chart is between a size XS and S but because of the cotton/rayon content I was fearful of shrinkage and did not want them to fit tight as these are a more casual look. They did not shrink to a noticeable degree. Material is on the lighter side for pants, I would consider these better for warmer seasons. Very comfortable and soft to wear. I disagree with some reviews that say pants have stretch. There is no elastic in material, and I don't consider cotton/rayon to be stretchy. Front pockets fit my entire hand (or cell phone), however back pocket is shorter.

Love these pants!! I'm sorry, my photos are not great. I'm just posting what I have in my personal gallery, not intended for review. Great wide leg cropped jeans I purchased these jeans in the color Cetacean blue, or dark blue. I bought both the size 4 and 6 bc I didn’t know which size I would be. For reference, I’m 5’4”, ~138-142 lbs. These are very stretchy! They have a lot of give, so I would probably size down from your usual non-stretch size jeans. I ended up keeping the size 4 (pictured). Material is good quality! (not amazing), but for the price, it’s good quality. The length hits just above my ankle. I have a lot of room in these jeans because of the stretch and wide leg style. Will be cute with boots and a belt or with sneakers for fall.

Comfy with a bit of stretch - but not the most flattering I was excited for these wide leg jeans - and overall - I'm happy with them. I am on the smaller end of medium - 5'6", 150lbs. I ordered a size 6 and they fit overall well - maybe just a bit tighter in the waist. My observations: PROS: * quality denim and well made. Not too heavy, but not too thin either. * stop at a great spot and flow well as I move around * wore to a sunny tournament and stayed cool - lots of airflow up into the pants which was great! CONS: * They have a nice leather patch on the waist that says 'GENLECK' - I just used a seam ripper and removed it. * The 'back end' photo was taken by my 9yo daughter - so her height/angle - but 'do these make my rear look funny'? Something just isn't great with the cut and fit of these pants (normally not a problem for me).

Great Stylish Jeans NOTE: I am a member of the Amazon Vine Program. I try out new products, and then provide an honest, unbiased review, based on my personal impressions and experience. I'm 5'6", so it's hard to see the "crop" effect in the pic I've posted with this review, but they fall just above my ankles. I've gotten lots of wear out of them already this summer, because they go with everything, and it's so easy to dress them up or dress them down. Excellently constructed, and comparable to any pair of jeans I would find in a high-end department store, but for half the price. Highly recommend!

I was going back and forth on how I would rate this, four or five stars, but went with five. These are definitely wide legged jeans and that’s what I ordered. However, they may be too wide legged for me and that’s why I was thinking of rating them 4, but that’s not the fault of the jeans that I’m not sure to wearing wide legs jeans (been doing skinny jeans for way too long). As far as a nice pair of jeans at a reasonable price these are great. I’m 5’9” and around 150 lbs and ordered a size 8 in them (I always go up in jeans) and they fit well. The land right above my ankles, which is what I expected, the color is great, and they are wide. I’m just not used to me in wide legged jeans or pants, but as skinny jeans have been out for quite awhile it’s high time I get with it and get used to it. So, that’s why I’m eating them 5 stars. They’re great jeans at a great price.

Perfect length! These are great jeans! They are not Levi's but they are not the super cheap thin jeans material. It has a nice thickness and stretch to it. I'm so happy for the length as they fall right where they should. I've always had issues with jeans flooding my short stature. I ordered in a size 2 and the fit is perfect. For reference, I am 5ft 2 in, 120-125 lbs, athletic build, and size small/x-small, 0-2, 25-26 inch waist fits me best. So, do order to size. All the stitching and sewing is done well with no open seams, overlapping of material or loose hanging thread. There was a slight chemical smell which I corrected with a very hot wash cycle. I do recommend these jeans!

Super cute! These jeans are super cute and so comfortable. They fit perfectly - I would say that the size is accurate; I ordered a size 2 (I am 5'5" and weigh 106 lbs). They have just the right amount of stretchiness; they don't feel tight at all. They sit right at the belly bottom, so they are rather high waisted. They fit perfectly around the waist and are loose in the legs. They are cropped, so they look really nice with heels. These jeans are good quality. The stitching is great - I did not see any loose threads or other manufacturing errors. The pants have 2 pockets in back and 3 up front (2 regular sized and one of those tiny pockets no one ever uses). I really like the look of these jeans - very different from the straight, tight jeans I have been wearing. I definitely recommend these jeans - and the price is very reasonable!

Tighter Than Expected, Overall Nice Fit and Fabric I'm 5'4" and 38"x33"x39". According to the size chart the Size 12 would fit best at my waist, but would have been baggy at the hips. I decided to purchase a 10 and a 12 to make sure of the fit. The 12 was obviously tight looking while still in the package, so I tried them on first—never even opened the size 10. The 12 fit very tight across the hips and thighs, but are not tight around my waist. Waistband rolls when I sit, but I don't tuck my shirts in as I did for the photo. Thick fabric for a stretch denim. Regular inseam length hits right above the back of my foot. Front pockets are very shallow, 2" deep. Followup note: These pants are difficult to pull all the way up. I have to grab the waistband and hold it while I bend over to pull them upward up to 3 times. Once up they stay pretty well, but I wish the waistband was a little tighter. Basically, every slim-cut stretchy denim pant I have tried has this same tight hips and loose waist fit problem.

Relaxed fit, runs a bit loose and long Ordered two pair of these, an 8L (recommended by Amazon) and a 6L (because 6 is my usual size in pants). For reference, I’m 5’7” and 125 lbs, abt a 37” hip. The 8L were big, boxy, too long and too loose (wrong size); the 6L were a better fit—definitely not tight, though at least 4” too long. Returned both these Long-sized Amazon orders, and since Amanda regular 6 was not available, I ordered a pair in that size elsewhere. The reg 6 fits better, though still longer than other regular-sized jeans I own (a plus—can launder without worry). The Amanda style is a more loose-fit style than skinny jeans, and definitely look looser on me than on the model. But they are a higher-waisted, stretchy style, esp good for when you’re going to be doing a lot of sitting (eg. travel). *UPDATE: The 6R seemed to stretch out and get even looser after a few days of travel wearing last week. Based on reviews by others my size or larger, I ordered a size 4R (Zermatt), even though Amazon was now suggesting I order 6L. The 4R is actually the best fit in this Amanda style, and is still plenty long. *PHOTOS are of the 4R. Third time’s a charm! (These pants did need to be washed right away due to strong chemical smell.)

Great jeans... but don't do what I did! lol! Returned another brand to try these. Bought them in white. I'm 5'6.5 and 138 pounds (feel my normal weight should be 130 to 133... I'm working on it, lol!) Anyway, I bought the size 6 short (I'll explain the short part at the end). I could button them, but when you run around in leggings most days, normal pants can feel a little too snug. I thought, "I know! I'll wet them and stretch them over a chair!" No! Bad idea! the next day, they were huge! I wore them and had to pull them up constantly, lol! So I wet them again and put them in the dryer. Fortunately, they tightened up - but that's the point. JUST wear them! Sit in them, walk around. They will loosen up on their own and feel fine! I even thought the thigh felt a little too snug... no... they're fine. Just needed to break them in!The reason I bought short (I normally don't) was just because I heard they ran long... and in the summer, I like to roll up my jeans - especially white ones - just for the look (I like them casual and relaxed) - so I thought if I get short, less rolling and I was right. They're just what I wanted!

True to size After reading a lot of reviews, I took a chance and ordered the pants. I have an apple shape with no butt, so it's hard to find pants that will go around my waist. These fit great! I'm 5'9" and 183 lbs. I bought 18 long and I'm very had happy. If I had a smaller waist, I would have bought smaller size. I like the give in the stretchy fabric. Very comfortable.

Buy these Jeans cause They're Great I bought a size larger than I usually wear because I like wearing roomy jeans. A size 14 would have been more snug and fit perfectly but I like clothes that are comfortably loose. I am 5'7. I immediatly tried them on -- perfect fit for my taste -- then I put them in the washer first and dryer 2nd. They didn't shrink at all! The dryer (with a dryer sheet) actually made them even softer. The color is dark blue and they didn't fade onto the other clothes in the washer at all. A white pillow case came out white! They are comfortable and when I stand up the legs touch my shoe tops. The picture was taken while setting with my legs stretched out.
Fits great, very comfortable I love these jeans. I’ve tried on so many pairs lately and these fit perfectly. I’m 5 foot 10 and weigh 170 pounds and bought an 8 long.

Way better than I had expected Usually I’ll buy some fashion nova jeans in a 15 for $30 and they’ll show up a little tight but stretch out in a few hours. Few months in they’ll tear at the thigh anti-gap lol. A coworker convinced me to get jeans at Target in July. Got 3 pairs of “Levi’s” in a 14 (because they’re stretchy) for like $40/ea. Literally wearing these things to work every day because that’s the only thing I can do through this apocalypse. Some how a pair of these garbage pants ripped in a month. A MONTH. So. I’m on amazon and I see these here bad boys on sale for $9 and I figure I should honestly just buy six pairs and call it a night. Just in case though I upped it to a size 16. If they run small, they’ll fit. If they run regular, I’ll wear a belt. Well guess what. They run regular. The delivery lady Just dropped them off and I pulled one on and these bad boys are so comfortable, but I ordered them big lol. Anyway, I’ll wear a belt to work. I got two pairs but I came back to buy two more, but the sale is over, sad day, but not that sad. They seem way sturdier than the ones on fashion nova AND these pants not only have 4 1/2 Real Pockets but I can also fit my entire hand in the front ones. Heck, I can fit a couple TV remotes in the front pockets. I’m definitely coming back, and even though the $9 sale is over, full price is still a steal compared to Target’s “Levi’s” For reference I’m about 5’5 and 197lbs this week but I’m kindof dense in the bones. A lot of my weight is butt, a lot of it is thighs...these jeans don’t quite do the butt justice but that’s definitely on me because I purposely ordered them too big haha if I figure out how to update this review, I’ll edit to add pictures when I buy the right size

Love Gloria Vanderbilt Amanda jeans , I have worn this brand for years . They fit my body better than any others . I have another patterned jeans from GV and have been searching for another. Finally found it on Amazon got them by using try before you buy. On receiving them, I tried them on immediately sent them to the washing machine turned wrong side out and wash them and dry them. I tried them on again and they were still perfect. No shrinkage immediately notified Amazon and Purchased absolutely love them and get many compliments whenever I wear them I am 5 foot six and 165 pounds I purchased a 12 short and it works for me.

Dont Think This Style's Good for Petites/Shorts, Otherwise Nice Im 5'5" a little over 35.5" in waist, 45" hips, 24" diameter thighs and 16" diameter legs/calves, no thigh gap. What drew me to these pants were that they were high waisted and on a lightning deal for like 20 bucks. I didnt think much about the cut beyond the waist but figured tapered legs would be attractive. I think the deal breaker for me is the style. They look kind of like straight legs on me, and since I have large thighs it's just more fabric than I want in the leg area. I dont like how the fabric bunches behind my knee and though this isn't entirely on the jeans I don't think they're doing my bum any favors. I did go a size larger than i probably should have with the 16, I just hated the idea of the top of the jeans pinching my stomach. I also got the regular instead of the short but I dont think the tapers sharp enough for the extra length to make a difference here, meaning to give shape to my legs. I think the taper's only really attractive if your thighs arent super disproportionate to your legs. Maybe like 2 or 3 inches max wider diameter in the thighs in relation to your calves. At least for the fit I want. I was gonna send these back in exchange for some 14's but I think I'll have to go with one of the other cuts to get the fit I'm looking for. Besides the fit I think these were a good buy for the price. Nice stretch and color. not as thick as some denim blends, a bit thicker than like denim leggings. Comfortable to move around in, liked fit at the waist. Better than most things you'd find between 20 - 30. If I had to recommend based on fabric and comfort vs leg fit I'd recommend.

Decreasing quality I love the Amanda jeans and have worn them for years. I bought the lighter Amanda jeans from this listing two years ago, and the darker Portland Wash jeans last week. The finishing on the new pair is so much more raggedy than the older pair after many washings! The fabric content is slightly different: the lighter pair is 73% Cotton, 25% Polyester, 2% Elastane and the darker is 72% Cotton, 26% Polyester, 2% Elastane. The first is made in Jordan, the second in Kenya. I did buy two other pairs in the two year interim, and I returned them as the “fits at natural waist” was uncomfortably well above my belly button. The pairs I kept both needed to be hemmed, but I’m used to that. I’m 5’4” with a 29” inseam. The Short length is too short at 28”, and the Regular length is too long at 30”. This pair of jeans fit comfortably and the darker color is more dressy, which is what I wanted. I’m glad I have a pair I can keep!

Grace Karin does it again! I am normally a size 4 and ordered the small with hesitation. The size chart recommended the small but I detest overly tight waist bands. I am 5’6” and 128 lbs and I am pleased with the fit. My photo is not the best but I wanted to show the leg split isn’t as high as indicated on the model. In fact, it’s hard to see at all until I sit down and cross my legs. The waist is 95% elastic with just the front section lying flat. The ties are mostly for looks but allow a bit of cinching, if needed. The fabric is lightweight, like summer chiffon, and not the best option for cold weather but will be perfect for spring summer. I classify the pants as dressy-perfect for the office or church. The pants will need to be de-wrinkled upon arrival. I have never been disappointed in the quality of this brand and I have many pieces.

Pear shaped? Then pass I guess I didn’t realize these are ankle pants, and boy are they ankle pants! With the waist o the pants pulled up to my true waist (now they are supposed to fit) they fall at about 4 inches above my ankle. If you pull them down so that they are not ankle pants, you have a strange “sagging” look and crotch line (second pic). I am 5’4” and bought a medium at first based on the pant measurements on this site. Those were too big, so I bought the small. Those fit right, but I’m not sure I like them. I’m a “pear” shape (more weight in my bottom half than my top half) and these pants REALLY add some weight to your hips and butt. It doesn’t look bad in pics, but makes me look very disproportioned in person. They are so comfy, but I’m not sure the bare ankles and chunky rear end are worth the comfort. Just not for me.

Super cute, thick material and a bit long. Love these wide leg pants! They are soft and super comfortable. The fit is a bit large for me, as is the length a bit long. I got an X-Large which is mostly my normal size. Pretty sure a large would have been a better fit for me with these. I have some platform shoes that I think will alleviate the length issue for me if I wear them out of the house. I am mostly a barefoot or slippers person at home, so I do not think I will wear them much at home. I really like them a lot, just wish they were a little shorter. I am 5' 4", so I often have an issue with pants being too long. It did say the inseam was 30" so I did not think they would be as much too long as they are. That said they are super cute. The material has a nice, weighty thickness to it and feels good when wearing them. No scratchiness, just nice and soft. I feel that these pants will be good for keeping warm in the colder months, along with being very comfy while wearing them. They have two nice sized pockets. I'm recommending these pants as they are nice quality and well made.

Comfy, Cute, & Cozy! These pants are exactly as expected. Love the Deep Blue color - it is the same in person as in the picture. The jeans are warm and fuzzy on the inside. They are comfortable to wear and are insulating, without being too thick. They are made of 70% cotton, 28.5% polyester, and 1.5% elastane. I appreciate the elastane in them, because it gives some stretch to the garment and makes them easy to move in. Even the first time putting these jeans on, they were easy to move in. These can be machine washed on a gentle cycle and line dried. They can be dry cleaned if needed, but not bleached. Appreciate the easy care of these pants. The front pockets are deep too, which is great. I am 5'6 and typically wear jeans with a 29 to 30" waist and the size M fits well in both length and width! I love that these are high waist and they weren't too tight or too big. The dark blue color of the pants makes them so they can be paired with a wide assortment of tops, jackets, coats, and shoes to create unique outfits. I am excited to wear them this fall and winter.

Great fabric! These pants are made well, the fabric is very stretchy but thick enough to provide enough structure so they don't look like yoga pants. The high waist is the most comfortable that I've ever worn. These pants look good with my flats and my heels so they're very versatile ! I washed in cool water on the normal cycle and tumble-dried and they didn't shrink, pill or wrinkle. No iron needed. So they're very easy care. I ordered medium and I'm 5'6" 155 lbs and they fit great. They accommodate my bigger legs and the waist could be smaller but the pleats make them very flattering. One thing I noticed when wearing is the front creases when you sit, even for a few minutes, as you can see in the last picture. But it's a small price to pay for such comfortable pants. I'd definitely order again and recommend them!

Wonderful pants! Really nice medium-weight fabric with a class cut and sheen. I'm not a super-huge fan of the butt pockets on the front, but I'm seeing the trend often so I guess it's a thing. I'm 6 ft tall, 139 lbs and I'm wearing Medium. I probably could have sized down to a Small because the waist is fairly loose. They're a nice shade of grey -- and grey is on-trend for FW24/25, as is the looser, medium-wide straight leg. I'm happy to have these in my rotation. They're nice enough to go from day to evening if you're wearing a top and shoes that can make that transition; these can easily carry a sparkly top and dressy shoes.

Really nice looking capris Written by my wife: I am 5 ft tall, about 125 lbs, and I usually wear size small, but on the measurement charts that were provided I was more of a size medium so that’s what I ordered. They fit well and are really comfortable. The fabric is not cheap and it has a good amount of stretch, thank goodness, because I’m curvy and need the accommodation. The almond milk color is a light khaki in person and will coordinate with many tops. I think the description of both dressy and casual is accurate, depending on how you dress them up or down. Some reviewers complained about the wrinkles just out of the package, but I always wash my clothing before wearing, and after washing and hang drying my pants were wrinkle free and ready to wear.

Great color, consider sizing up These are a fun, light weight goucho-style pant. I am in love with the cool toned 'blue grey' color, which is going to be my neutral alternative to olive bottoms that tend to be too warm-toned for my other favorite clothes/colors. I like the pockets, and the (matching!) zipper and button waist, with an elastic band at the back. There are belt loops all around the waist the lay very flat, so won't gap out be super obvious if you choose not to wear a belt. The stitching and seams are fine, but not super quality. The fabric is a (very wrinkly) linen style, that I found comfortable and not as itchy as another review had mentioned. I like the length on my 5'2" frame. I do think the size chart is a bit inaccurate in the US numerical listing but not necessarily in the measurements, which can get tricky; I followed my hip measurements so I didn't get too much fabric gathering which made the waist just a little tighter than I'd prefer, though the elastic in the rear waist helps. Overall I like these pants, but I think they might be priced a little high for their quality ($33.99 at time of review). I'm excited to see how much wear I can get out of them before they fall apart this spring/summer.

Cute slacks I love these slacks. The fit and length is just right, being somewhat baggy. I like to wear them rolled up as it looks better for someone of my stature: short, 5ft 2in, 120-125 lbs athletic build. I ordered in a size small and usually size small or xsmall fits me best. The material is neither too thick or too thin, just perfect for the fall weather. All the stitching and sewing is done well with no open seams, overlapping of material or loose hanging thread. I do recommend these pants if you are looking for something chic but yet still comfortable and casual.

Nice Cropped Slacks I ordered these pants for a bachelorette event. It seems we will have custom T-shirts that we have to wear with hot pink or "Barbie" pink pants/bottoms. I wasn't sure what look I wanted to go for so I ordered these when I saw them because they were the right color & something a bit more dressy than jeans. I ordered size Large, which is my usual size. I might have been able to get away with a Medium as they are fairly loose & the waist has elastic. I'm tall (5'9") & was worried that they'd be way too short but when I look at them in the full-length mirror, I think the length really isn't bad. The material is light enough for summer. I'm not sure if I'll be wearing these for the event but I do see myself wearing them for something else. They are cute & well made & kind of different from what I normally wear, so It'll be fun to mix things up.

Not on My List of Favorites The - GRAPENT Wide Leg Jeans for Women Trendy High Waisted Flare Stretchy Bell Bottom Denim Pants Baggy Raw Hem Trouser Jeans – are not on my list of favorite jeans. For starters, they have a strong chemical smell that after a few days left to air out, is still very strong. I cannot wait to launder them. Before I do that, I want to cut from the hem at least 2 inches if I intend to wear with heels. If I wear flats, I’ll need to take off about 3 or 4 inches. I’m 5’4” tall. The fit is decent in that these jeans are not too tight nor are they too big. However, because the fabric is extremely stretchy, these pants keep falling down…and I can put them on and take them off without the need to unbutton and unzip. I’m hoping that after they are laundered, this will be less of an issue. Overall, I don’t think this style of jeans is well suited to my fuller figure frame and I would not buy these again. For sizing reference, my waist measures 37”, and my hips measure 48”. I purchased a size XXL.

Definitely warm! I am impressed with these jeans! The color is great, and the fit is size accurate according to the sizing chart. They definitely are high waisted. The pants have a nice but slight stretch. The inside of the legs are super soft and the pants definitely keep you nice and toasty. I definitely recommend if you are going to be outside in the cold, say, for a night football game. They may be a little too toasty in a crowded place with heat, unless you just are cold natured. The pants are an average length (they were long on me as I’m 4”11) but will pair nicely with boots. You can’t really roll them up at the ankle as the inside lining shows. I do wish they left a little room without lining so they can be rolled.

Almost Too Perfect. I don’t think anyone is loving the elastic band on these pants, but that is my only complaint. I like my jeans tight around my butt/thighs, which is hard because I don’t much have a butt or thighs. But I love this fit and they feel like wearing comfy sweatpants. I’m 5’7”, 115lbs, 27”Waist, 34”Hips, 18” Thighs and got an XS. There’s a bit of a loose area in front, but it doesn’t look weird and makes space for more curvy figures. The fleece does add a scotch of bulk, but I need all the bulk I can get on my skinny legs. The cut is tighter in the lower legs than the seat and thighs. Probably not great if you’ve got larger or athletic calves, but the stretch is fantastic. I put these in the dryer on low to tighten up just a little, no problems. The fleece isn’t terribly thick like another pair I have, but is still warm and super soft. The blue is a nice wash on the less faded side than some. I very much recommend these fleece jeans to pretty much anybody.

Comfy, Gen X High School Jeans I have been living in these jeans. I love them. They remind me of the jeans I used to wear in high school in the mid/late 90s. I am 5’6”, 165lbs, carry most of my weight in my hips/thighs/booty, and I ordered a Large. These were a great buy and I highly recommend them!

Cute but waist is unforgiving! I love the throwback style of these jeans and was looking forward to trying them. I have really mixed feelings about them though. First, the fabric has some stretch to it which is great EXCEPT the waist doesn't give much at all and despite ordering a large based on the size chart provided, these were torture to get on. The waist itself was okay once I finally got them past my hips but it was a STRUGGLE! Note that my hips are not disproportionately large compared to my other measurements. If they had a zipper, they would be perfect or if the waist band had more stretch to them, that would work as well. In terms of the length, they are a little bit long on me at 5'4" but they work with a chunky heel. They just fit as longer jeans and not ankle length as suggested in the pictures. Otherwise, the fabric is really comfortable but I'm not sure they will last very long. It's a little hard to see from my picture but the seam that goes directly down the front of the leg seems a little fragile and I don't think it would take much to tear apart. In summary, I will wear these jeans when I'm feeling motivated to do the work of getting them on but personally, I wouldn't order again if given the chance with the way the sizing is.

Loving these jeans I’m 5’5,” 165 lbs and my measurements are 44(38/40C)bust, 34/35 waist & 44 hips. Per the size guide provided I ordered the XL. After trying, I could’ve gone down a size, but I really like the loose fit of these - they fit how they appear on the model, similar to a boyfriend fit. In my image I’m wearing 2.5” clogs. I love the look. These are super comfortable and the elastic waistband is wide and not constricting at all. The legs are wide but not exaggerated. After washing, these did shrink a bit, but mostly in length, but they still cover the tops of my feet. Fortunately, these pants have a generous hem, approx 3.” So if I ever wanted to wear these with even higher platforms, I could easily let the hem down. These are perfect weight for Fl. I can easily wear these in warm weather or with a sweater when it becomes cooler. I’m super happy with these and I definitely recommend. I also like that the brand, GRAPENT, has an Amazon store. Once I find a brand that suits me, I love to see what else they offer.

Almost great… I am 5 foot four, 115 pounds for reference and thankfully the length on these jeans is just right. Comfortable, Do Not Survive Washing Well When I first opened these jeans, the "raw hem" was not super noticeable. After a single wash, the amount of fraying at the hem did not bode well for the longevity of these jeans. I suspect each wash will get a heavier and heavier amount of fraying. If you like a cleaner look, you can trim them up again. I intend to wear them with sandals and the strings tickle my feet, so I will trim them up. They are marketed as cropped jeans, but at 5'3" with short legs, they are really just regular jeans for me. They fit well and are comfortable. The large flat pockets on the back are not the most flattering as they make your bottom look droopy. I sized up from what the listing recommended. I usually wear a 4 or 6, but do not like my jeans tight. I am happy that I did. I have a bit of a gap at the back around the waist, but these fit appropriately through the hips and thighs. Overall, I like the fit, but the quality is questionable.

oversized and soft This review is for the GRAPENT Wide leg palazzo pants, xx-large, dark blue. I usually wear an XXL in pull on pants, 17/18 in jeans, and XL (XXL is usually a little too big) in leggings. Approximate measurements are 41” waist and 51” hips, 5’6” and a 30 inch inseam reaches just to the floor on me. These pants are a delight to wear. They are unbelievably soft, thin, stretchy, and flowy. The style is meant to be oversized so definitely don’t size up even if you want a loose fit. The size chart was based on body measurement and not the dimensions of the pants. It says that XX-Large, US size 20 - US 22, waist 36.5 - 38”, hips 47 - 48.5”. My waist and hips are bigger than that and the pants still are a flowy fit on me. The waist has a few inches of smocking and is very stretchy. The fabric has a good horizontal stretch to it. These pants drag on the ground for me. I wouldn’t expect this with a 29” inseam but I measured and they are indeed 29 (maybe 29.25) inches. However even with the waist an inch or two above my belly button (which is perfect for me) the crotch sits a little low. It looks and feels good but that’s why the leg length is too long for me. They look like the could be a straight wide leg but are actually about 16” at the thigh and 20” at the hem. The pockets are huge and stretchy and don’t lay completely flat across the front but rather droop out a little at the top. As I mentioned, these pants are delightfully comfortable. They are a little too oversized and long for me to want to wear as everyday “real” pants. Instead I’ll wear them primarily as loungewear that I’m OK with wearing out of the house for something like running an errand.

Comfy; stretchy I’m 5’3”, 135lbs., normally wear an 27/28 or 6/7. I carry my weight in my bum/thigh/lower belly. I ordered a size small. Wasn’t expecting to like these as much as I do! They run a little on the big side, but in a good way. They are a loose fit and have a nice stretch, but they aren’t too loose and baggy. I would say order the size you normally wear; no need to size up. They’re totally comfy due to the stretch. They are def high-waisted. I don’t always look that flattering in high-waisted pants, but I feel good in these! My legs are short, so these look more like high waters on me rather than a capris, but I like the look with boots. But if you have short legs and want them shorter, keep in mind you may need to hem them. Planning to hang dry since they are quite stretchy and I don’t want to damage the elastic. They aren’t the best/thickest quality, which means they likely won’t last too long, but I bet they’ll be comfy when the weather starts to warm up. I was quite happy that I ordered these. We will see how they hold up!

They run a little on the big side, but in a good way. They are a loose fit and have a nice stretch, but they aren’t too loose and baggy. I would say order the size you normally wear; no need to size up. They’re totally comfy due to the stretch. They are def high-waisted. I don’t always look that flattering in high-waisted pants, but I feel good in these! My legs are short, so these look more like high waters on me rather than a capris, but I like the look with boots. But if you have short legs and want them shorter, keep in mind you may need to hem them. Planning to hang dry since they are quite stretchy and I don’t want to damage the elastic. They aren’t the best/thickest quality, which means they likely won’t last too long, but I bet they’ll be comfy when the weather starts to warm up. I was quite happy that I ordered these. We will see how they hold up!

Not exactly Ankle Pants - unless you are over 6’ These pants fit really well. Really well. They are a thinner denim than I usually wear, summer pants. They look nice as well, even though they do feel kind of cheap because of their thinner material. My biggest issue with these is that they are not ankle pants. The model picture shows them at about the ankle. These are more of a boot cut. I would guess they are about 1 or 2 inches higher than a “normal” pair of denim, more on the 1” vs. the 2. As if this was a “petit” pair. Cute with sandals. But, not ankle pants. I’m 5’7, if someone shorter put these on they would fit normally. Ankle pants for someone in the 5’10” range maybe…

Absolutely fantastic! As a size 5’9” around 200 lb woman, finding jeans can be difficult, but these are amazing. They fit like some of my high end jeans. Quality is great and there are lots of colors to choose from. I bought two colors, loved them both and then decided to get a third color!

Stylish and comfortable high-waist jeans but beware of the sizing and length! I have a lot of good things to say about these jeans! First off, the high waist is just perfect, it cinches the waist beautifully, creating a flattering silhouette. I also love the wide-leg style; it gives a lengthening effect that makes my legs look longer and adds a touch of retro chic to my look. The square pockets are a unique and also have a bit of a vintage vibe, which I adore. The classic denim is another highlight. It’s thick and stretchy, very comfortable,great for cooler autumn and winter weather. Plus, they’re super easy to style. I find they pair especially well with crop tops, creating a trendy, well-put-together outfit. Despite the quality and style, though, I’m not entirely satisfied with the Fit. I always order a Medium, but these jeans run large. I need a belt to keep them up. Also, the length is a bit much for me, given that all sizes have the same 30" inseam. At 5’2” and 116 pounds, they’re too long for my frame. If you’re on the shorter side and don’t want to hem them, keep in mind that they might be a bit lengthy. Overall, for style and quality, these jeans deserve a solid 5/5. If I were taller, the small size would have fit perfectly. Just be mindful of the sizing if you’re petite!

Comfortable and stylish jeans! I am very pleasantly surprised by these jeans. They are extremely comfortable and they look great too! I think jeans can be tricky to get right. They’re either too tight or too baggy or they’re too long or too short but these are “Goldilocks jeans” because they are just right 👏. The material is soft and stretchy so they have some give in them. I ordered a small and they fit perfectly around the hips and waist without feeling tight. The product description said the inseam was 30” and I knew this was the exact length I needed when wearing boots with a heel. I’m 5’4” but I do have short legs so although these work great for me, they may be too short for many people of the same height. If I had any criticism, it would be that the stitching wasn’t great in some areas but I’ve been able to tidy it up using a pair of small nail scissors. Overall, I couldn’t be happier with how these jeans look and feel! And, you certainly can’t beat the price point because jeans can be expensive. I highly recommend 😊.

wash first - but recommend! Unfortunately I didn't take a picture of the packaging, however, they come rolled in a plastic bag. The problem is that they were "moist" and therefore had a smell which is why I removed a star.For reference, I am 5'3, wear a very loose size 12, tight size 10 (US sizes). I ordered a medium which according to the size chart, this should be for US sizes 8-10. I ordered a Medium as an incentive to push me to lose weight/fit into these pants by the end of the year. As you can see by the picture, they fit now - love it!! They are NOT tight, they are loose, comfortable and can bend with ease. They are VERY stretchy and while I don't necessarily like how they look on me yet, BUT you can see that they are very well made.The stitching is great, the pockets are very deep. I put my iPhone16 (for reference) in the front pocket and bent forward and it didn't come out. I place it in the back pocket and it moved up a bit but 90% of my phone was still in the pocket so it did not fall out which is always a plus! The pants are a bit long but I am short (again, I am 5'3) so short heels/boots will fix the issue. The pockets don't "bulge open" because of the material and they are really soft and comfortable!Highly recommend but wash these first - no, they didn't shrink.

Thin Fabric, fairly see through These are very high waisted pants. I ordered the light beige which is actually a pretty yellowy cream color. I feel the fabric is too thin for this type of pant. In this light color you can see the front pockets through the fabric. I was surprised they were so long, I thought they were shorter. I’m 5’8” and they were a regular length on me. I like that the waist band in front is smooth, not elastic. But for me, there was too much fabric at the butt. Almost looks like I have a diaper on!? The elastic is really tight. I’d prefer less fabric at the back and looser elastic. I wouldn’t recommend these in the light color but the darker colors are likely fine. I ordered my usual size M.

I had high hopes for these pants. They arrived pretty well packaged, and the color is nice. The snaps on the cargo pockets work well. However, the pants were a bit too long for my wife. She is 4'11". I know they are supposed to balloon out a bit but these are too long even when lifting them up and pulling the draw strings. I suppose if you're a bit taller or have long legs then it's fine. These are really basic pants though. They have no branding or tags on them. I couldn't even find a size/care tag inside the pants. There's a torn piece of fabric hanging on a draw string on the right pants leg but I couldn't figure out where it came from. It could have happened when it was manufactured. The material is thin and my wife said it did feel comfortable. Overall, I wouldn't really recommend these pants if you can spring for similar pants with a bit higher price tag but also better quality.

Comfy and versatile These pants are light-weight, quick drying, and quite comfortable. I do like how the nip in at the waist and then are lose throughout the legs. I enjoy how deep and functional the pockets are. I could fit my cellphone, no problem. They are very swishy so these aren't pants you can sneak around in, but that doesn't bother me. I prefer to wear the cuff untightened because the pants are long enough to hit me at the heel, but I appreciate the options. I think these would be decent hiking pants as well. I am 5'4, 125 pounds, with 38" hips and 27" waist and although the small was the right size, a good fit, and the waistband still had room to stretch, I was definitely aware of the waistband while wearing.

I wanted them to be my new favorite loungers, but they’re not. I’ve been looking for something other than leggings to wear around the house, so I decided to give these a try. I’m 5’6”, 190 pounds, size 12/14 in pants. According to the manufacturer’s sizing chart, I fit in their size large which is what I ordered. I’ve worn them around for a few days, here are my thoughts.What I liked.• The fabric is soft and drapes nicely.• The fabric doesn’t get stretched out and baggy.• They are machine wash cold, tumble dry low. I laundered mine that way and they came out great.• The pants are solidly constructed and designed well. The manufacturer went the extra mile to add a yoke on the back of the pants which creates a more flattering fit.• They have 2 on-seam pockets that are big and deep. I could securely carry my iPhone 15 Pro Max in them.• They are comfortable, mostly.• They fit, mostly. I liked the 29” inseam, which hit me below the ankle. For hanging around the house, the length was good. I wasn’t stepping all over the hem, especially going up and down the stairs.What I didn’t like.• The fabric is a little odd. Yes, it’s soft, but it is more of a heavy knit.• The fabric is hot. Because of the loose, wide leg I thought they’d be nice and cool during our heatwave. They were not.• The pants were not a great fit through the hips on me. They were just a tiny bit snug. As a result, I preferred the look of a long, oversized t-shirt over them. They also seemed a little short in the rise (crotch).Overall, I think these sweatpants fall squarely in the “they’re OK” category. I wasn’t crazy about the feel and weight of the fabric, or the fit. However, they are well made and do launder great. I’m giving them 4 stars.

good comfortable fit for large thighs These pants are roomy and comfortable. I have large thighs and these complement me nicely, evening my legs a bit. The length is a bit long for me, but I expected this, since I am 5'2". With heeled shoes its not much of a problem, but with flats they might be a tad long. Overall, I am pleased with the style and fit.

Fit As Described I am 5' 7" and between 125-130 lbs. and the size Medium was recommended, and fits appropriately. The fabric is comfortable; it's not flannel-soft, but it's not scratchy. It's not see-through, but because it is clingy, and shows one's... hills and valleys, shall we say?... I'd wear tights beneath the pants if I was wearing them outside the house (which I could see doing, for casual occasions.) They would make great around-the-house lounge pants, with an appearance that is nicer than sweats. Think of them as cocktail sweats. :) If you have pets that shed, the fabric does seem to be a magnet for pet hair.

I’m going to start this off by saying make sure you size down! I am normally a size 4 but decided to order a size 2 once I saw it is made of 2% elastane. Comfortable, Roomy Jeans with Some Stretch I ordered these jeans in size Medium in the color Medium Blue. They have a recycled look to them. They are cut roomy in the thighs and legs, which I find comfortable. They are made of 76% Cotton/26% Polyester/2% Elastane so they have some give to them. After laundering them three times, they haven't noticeably shrunk. For reference, I'm a long-waisted/short-inseamed 5' 4", 124 lbs, 29" waist and 38" hips. The length of these on me is at the longest side of OK. I think I could have ordered these in a size small, as they are a little baggy and the waist slips down a bit if I don't wear a belt. They have a strange smell that hasn't completely washed out after 3 washings, but I suspect it will eventually. Overall, I like these comfy jeans and I think they look kind of boho stylish.

Casual capris with beach-y fabric; a little prone to wedgies I got these capris pants so that I'd have some casual summer capris in a fun color. SIZE/FIT: I got an XXL in the olive green, and they fit as expected (the waist is a little snug on me, but I knew that going in, since I have a 42" waist - but they're pretty comfortable and stretchy regardless). I'm 5"6', about 220 lbs. The capris pant stops about 5-6 inches above my ankle. APPEARANCE: I love the color of the fabric; it's a lovely, light, slightly muted green. I like how the capris look from the front and sides. On the backside, however, the fabric kept getting caught up on my behind, giving my a slight wedgie appearance. For that reason, I took off a star. FEATURES: These pants have nice sized, open pockets, big enough for a phone or wallet. I like the wide, elastic waist. FUNCTION/FABRIC: The fabric of these pants is light and kind of crinkled; they would be great for the beach or on a hot summer day. Overall, I'm very happy with these pants, aside from the wedgie issue.

Make sure you are wearing the right underwear! These are soft and comfortable. However, with the high waist and smooth material good fitting underwear is ESSENTIAL, also make sure your shirt is tucked in perfectly, or wear one that needs no tucking. These have good stretch, I wear an 8 curvy L cut in jeans, these are a medium they fit my waist and stretch to accommodate my large hips and my booty. I can totally see myself enjoying these in the next few months here in Florida as it will be nice and warm, like today it's January and 80. These are lightweight and don't have that slightly stiff feeling of a pure linen pair of pants, instead they almost have a silky tendency! Now, the only drawback for me as a busy mom, is not being able to throw these into the dryer with everything else. I did wash these and then draped them across the rack to dry before trying them on again for pictures, I didn't notice any shrinkage in regular washing! I think I might need another pair for variety's sake, instead of my beloved leggings. I'm 5'10" about 160, long torso, long thighs compared to calves, for reference 4 stars because I'm being persnickity about not being able to toss in the dryer on medium with everything else.

Easy Breezy If you’re a short girl like me and always looking at cropped pants wondering if they’ll be good “regular pants” on you, here you go: I’m 5’ tall and these are full length on me, just right for flat sandals in the summer. This pic is right out of the package; I’m not worried about the wrinkles, though — it’s kind of a given for this style of pants that you’ll have to iron or steam them before wearing. They’re a Medium and I normally wear a 6-8 pant, so I’d say true to size and the perfect throw-em-on thing for basically any occasion in the summer. I could get away with these at my office or going to dinner, running errands, whatever. They’re light and airy and the green is a great neutral. Also, yay for pockets!

Comfortable and cute Wasn’t sure I would like how they fit, but sure enough, it’s like so many of the other positive reviews, I do! Cute & Comfy, But Lose Around My Waist & Over My Hips Cute and Comfy would definitely define these pants. The pants exhibits exquisite quality providing maximum stretch and ease. Being around children I have to constantly bend down, pick up things and I am running errands. At moments like these, I prefer to be comfortable and want to make a fashion statement at the same time. I like the style of the pants and them being wide leg they look fine over my legs. Where they fall a bit big is by the waistline and the hips. I ordered a size small in white and the length of the pants is perfect since I'm 5'2, but they are not cropped in that sense, and I don't mind them being full length pants, I actually like them being that way. The pants waist does have loops so I can wear a belt fixing the issue of them being loose. I believe for someone who has a waist line of 27' or 28' inches wouldn't need a belt. The bottom opening of the pants is about 9' inches. I like that the pants have nice front pockets where you can easily slide your phone or a thin card wallet. I would recommend these pants because I find them to be comfortable and stylish overall.

Never know what you're gonna get It’s hard to find the right fit because I’m 5’2”, 30” inseam. Inverted triangle shape maybe? with a fuller waist. Because of this, when I order online, I get different sizes so I don't have to return and reorder all the time, but.... It’s exhausting. Normally, I wear a 6 or 8/28 in high waist jeans and 4 or 27/28 in lower rises. S-M in leggings. Inseam depending on style 25”-32”. All orders below I ordered 32” inseam and none were that. I included pics of 2 black (can't see1 of them and hard to see difference in color) and one of the ice blue.I ordered:4 (S) Ice Blue Fit was oh k, but afraid it might stretch to be too big because fabric is stretchy.6 (M) Ice Blue Too big all overThe small is more like a size 6. The fabric was decent quality, the color is a very light blueI ordered (XS) Lapis longing blue The fit in the butt was ok but bordering on too tight and uncomfortably tight in the waist. The color is med. dark blue with feathering …fading.. bleached? in the butt and on the front thighs. The fabric felt cheap. Thin, kind of crunchy or stiff but stretchy.I ordered(S) Always Black too big! Bigger than the 6(M) the color was very black, the fabric different from all the others which I kind of expected, but ok and the quality was good for the price. I liked these and was happy I had ordered the XS assuming they would be the winner or so I thought….(XS) Always Black, when I tried these, the fit ok except that the fabric felt thinner and the color had a slightly washed look to them and then the length was shorter than all of them! UGH!!! So frustrating!All sizes were shorter than the stated 32”. They varied between 30 and 31. So bummed because I liked them for the most part. I could accept the lesser quality if I could find one that had the qualities of three put together fabric of the ice blue, the smaller size and color of the Always black and then the right length of 32"! LOLMy favorite fabric and fit were the ice blue. Always black my favorite color, so I will try one more time and order XS 32” inseam Ice Blue and Always black and pray that’s what I get. I’ll try to update.****UPDATE:Ended up order the “Always Black” only in XS/32”So disappointing and frustrating. What I got was extreme faded black looked like they had been washed and again the inseam was not 32”! The last 3 photos are the newest pair! I mean come on!😞

Good fit lightweight denim When my husband and I sold everything we owned & moved to Mexico, I packed on almost 30 lbs. We overindulged in all the delicious food here, and stayed in Airbnbs for months with no access to a gym. As a result, none of my clothes fit anymore, so I needed something I could wear that was at least as flattering as possible given the current state of my body. Since my size 10 jeans were too tight, I decided to get a 12 in these. In the photos, I weighed about 180 lbs, 37 waist , 42.5 hips and they were the tiniest bit snug, but the spandex in the denim allowed me to confirm to my body instead of giving me muffin top. Since I'm 5'7" with long legs, I ordered the 32 inch inseam. The length is perfect for a low wedge heel on me. I love that these jeans are lightweight with some stretch. So much better than the stiff denim from the old days (I was born in the 70s, raised in the 80s). The denim back then was thick and so stiff if you gained an ounce jeans weren't wearable anymore, and even if you could squeeze into them, they dug into your skin and left indentions and red marks. This more modern, stretch denim is so much more comfortable to wear, and the lighter weight fabric is more bearable in hot climates like Mexico.

Keeping but there are things you should know I read all the reviews and went into this knowing that these are not high quality. What gave me hope was the curvy girls I saw that loved them. For reference I'm 5'5", 36/27/40 measurements, 138lb. The good: I don't have a gap in the back. (this never happens!) The length is perfect. (also never happens) They are a little stiff but mostly comfortable. My thighs and butt have room to breathe! The bad: The distressed look on these is so tacky. I'll try to mute it by using dye. The seams are awful and could need reinforcement in stress areas. Look at the belt loop placement. Couldn't be bothered to make them symmetrical. The hardware is cheap looking and you'll need to rub a wax candle on the zipper to prevent sticking. Overall if you're athletic build/curvy these are so worth it for the cut alone. I very rarely find pants that fit me. I want to cry. When these inevitably fall apart, I'm going to take them apart and sew a higher quality copy of them with fabric that has stretch. This is such a relief after years of skinny jeans and manufacturers that pretended we didn't have bones, muscle, and fat on our bodies. With those very human things, you can also have a small waist! I'm mostly happy and glad I bought them. Took two stars off because they won't last long and I shouldn't have to dye them and resew the belt loops right out of the box.

3 1/2 stars These jeans fall between 3 and 4 stars and I don't think I would buy again. The good: The fit is great. I am 5' 7" 135lbs and normally wear either a 4 or 6 in other brands. The small in these fit nicely. They are a little tighter around my waist than I'd like, but I do have a pretty big waist-to-hip ratio (menopause belly!) However, they are stretchy so are wearable. The length is perfect on me and they are wide in the leg without being super-wide and baggy like some brands I've tried. The bad: The denim is low quality and the fabric is stiff. I have washed and steam-ironed them to soften them up a bit but they are still pretty stiff. Also, on the pair I received, the distressing is uneven. I ordered the deep blue color and there were three parallel white lines that looked like scratch marks on one leg. Luckily, I have a deep blue felt pen so I lightly touched up the white lines and now they aren't visible. I am sure I will wear these but they won't be my go-to jeans.

Cheap-ish Fit me well, nice color, soft, even if somewhat in the thin side. However the seam at the very top is scratchy and I don’t want to always have to wear a shirt tucked in. They’re also just long enough for me (I’m almost 5’9”), and I’m worried they’ll shrink in the wash. I’m afraid to will be going back as I’m at the point where I want things to work for me, or it’s not worth it.

Soft and comfy I bought these in blue and love them. Very stretchy and comfy. I’m 5’7 and 155lbs and purchased a medium. I am disappointed that the second pair I ordered in the Pecan had white lines on the top. I think to make them look worn but it doesn’t look natural. In the ad photo the lines are not noticeable. I will be returning. Would love them if they didn’t have the lines. Very pretty color.

Nice Fabric, Comfortable, BUT Runs Big These pants are very comfortable to wear, and the fabric feels soft against my skin. I found it convenient that I could remove the wrinkles from packaging by simply putting them in the dryer for a few minutes, and they looked great afterward. The wide legs and drawstring waist give these pants a casual feel, but they can also be dressed up with a nice blouse. The elastic waist is very forgiving, so if you're in between sizes, the waistline has some give. I also appreciate that they have pockets, which is always a plus for me as they provide a place for my hands. As for the size, I ordered my usual medium size, but these pants feel and look a bit too big for me. There's too much material around my hips and backside, which isn't very flattering. If I order again, I'll definitely go down a size. (For size reference, I'm 5'6" and 124 lbs.) Other than the sizing issue, I really like these pants, and the fabric has a linen-like appearance. I've attached some photos to give you a closer look and hope that they help you out. Thank you!

Elastic is snug, no need for drawstring. I ordered a Medium for my 5’6” 138 pound pear shaped self. My waist measures 27” and these pants measure 28” (see photo). There is not much (any) wiggle room which makes the drawstring purely decorative. I was hoping for a looser fit so that I could control how tight the waist is because I don’t like the feeling of elastic tight enough to make marks. I love the color. The fabric is a nice weight. I haven’t washed them yet so we’ll see how that goes. I plan to hang them to dry and not use the dryer. These are pretty pants, but for me they are borderline too tight and my waist is the smallest part of my frame. They fit comfortably in the hips and have a flowy drape all the way to the ankle. If the drawstring were functional these high waisted linen pants would fit a wider variety of women’s body types which seems to me to be a good goal for the manufacturer to consider if they want to sell them to more customers.
